data.description:
Strip scheduler tasks; re-add and validate one-by-one.

Problem statement: Currently some scheduled tasks are failing or unused. Remove all scheduled task registrations and reintroduce them one at a time, validating each with tests. 

Users:
- Operators / Maintainers: want reliable scheduled tasks that don't fail or spam.
- Developers: need clear, testable scheduled task implementations and examples for adding new tasks.

Success criteria:
- Each removed scheduled task is reintroduced behind a dedicated work item with tests and docs.
- At least one high-value scheduled task (intake runner or wl-audit) is fully re-enabled and validated with end-to-end tests.
- Scheduler integration tests pass locally and in CI.
- All related documentation updated.

Constraints:
- Must not change external behavior of unaffected scheduler features.
- Avoid large refactors; prefer incremental, test-covered changes.
- Respect existing Worklog stages/status patterns.

Existing state:
- Several scheduler-related tasks exist (wl-audit, cleanup-runner, intake runner). Parent epic: AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD.
- Automated intake and related helper work items already created (see Related work).

Desired change:
- Remove all existing scheduled task registrations, then add them back one at a time as separate work items; implement, test, and document each before proceeding to the next.

Related work:
- AM-0MNU7MXP40060TW7: Automated intake scheduled task — implements intake-runner and selection/dispatch patterns.
- AM-0MNFSNJ3W00787T5: Task: wl-audit scheduler command — high-value scheduled task with existing work.
- AM-0MNFSNA5B004ULRI: Task: cleanup-runner scheduler command — re-add as its own task.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
- Q: "Should any clarifying questions be asked during this intake?" — Answer (user instruction): "do not ask questions". Source: seed intake command. Final: yes (do not ask questions).

Risks & assumptions:
- Risk: scope creep from reintroducing tasks together; mitigate by creating individual work items and gating each with tests.
- Assumption: existing scheduler code paths are testable locally.

Implementation locations: src/ampa/scheduler.py, src/ampa/scheduler_executor.py, src/ampa/scheduler_helpers.py


Related work (automated report):
- AM-0MNU7MXP40060TW7: Automated intake scheduled task — implements intake-runner selection, dispatch and assignment patterns; useful example for re-adding intake runner.
- AM-0MNFSNJ3W00787T5: Task: wl-audit scheduler command — high-value scheduled task with existing investigation and tests; a candidate to re-enable early.
- AM-0MNFSNA5B004ULRI: Task: cleanup-runner scheduler command — existing cleanup pattern to reintroduce as separate task.
- src/ampa/scheduler.py, src/ampa/scheduler_executor.py: scheduler code locations to inspect when reintroducing tasks.

data.description:
Problem statement

The scheduled `gh-import` task (AM-0MNFSNCPY006HJ2W) that runs `python -m ampa.run_gh_sync import` is unreliable. Re-implement or fix it so it consistently imports GitHub issues on schedule, conforms to the current scheduler schema, and provides clear logs for operators.

Users

- Operators and maintainers who rely on automated GH issue imports to keep Worklog in sync with GitHub.
- Release and release-management engineers who depend on up-to-date issue data.

User stories

- As an operator, I want the gh-import task to run every 30 minutes and import new GitHub issues so Worklog reflects upstream issues without manual action.
- As a maintainer, I want the task to finish within 5 minutes and be configurable via scheduler descriptors so it is observable and safe to run.

Success criteria

- gh-import is registered in the scheduler store with a descriptor that validates against the current scheduler schema (includes `agent` when required).
- Task runs at a 30-minute frequency by default and completes within the configured timeout (<= 300 seconds) under normal load.
- Task logs are written to the scheduler logs and show successful runs and error cases; errors are retried with exponential backoff (max 3 attempts) for transient failures.
- Unit and integration tests cover import, rate-limit handling, and error/retry paths; tests pass in CI.
- All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

Constraints

- Must follow scheduler schema as documented in `src/ampa/scheduler_schema.md` (fields like `frequency_minutes`, `max_runtime_minutes`, `metadata`, and optional `agent`).
- Max runtime should be 5 minutes (configurable via `max_runtime_minutes`).
- Change must be idempotent: re-running scheduler or intake should not cause duplicated imports.

Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Scope creep — additional features (e.g., full GitHub sync, issue mapping rules) may be requested. Mitigation: record additional features as separate work items and keep this task focused on reliable import and tests.
- Risk: Rate limiting from GitHub API causing partial imports. Mitigation: implement rate-limit handling and retries with backoff; add tests to simulate rate limits.
- Risk: Descriptor/schema drift — scheduler descriptors that previously worked may fail schema validation. Mitigation: validate descriptor against `src/ampa/scheduler_schema.md` and run descriptor validation in CI.
- Assumption: run_gh_sync import mode is the correct integration point; if upstream changes require rework, the effort estimate will increase.

Existing state

- Scheduler schema docs exist at `src/ampa/scheduler_schema.md` showing a `gh-import` example using `python -m ampa.run_gh_sync import`.
- There is a current work item AM-0MNFSNCPY006HJ2W titled "Task: gh-import scheduler command" assigned to Map and staged as `idea`.
- Related work items: AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD (parent epic), AM-0MNFSNFUS000TI5B (gh-push sibling), AM-0MNFSNRND0018OL4 (pr-monitor) and AM-0MNU7MXP40060TW7 (Automated intake scheduled task) contain patterns and examples to reuse.

Desired change

- Review `src/ampa/run_gh_sync.py` and `tests/test_run_gh_sync.py`, update or re-implement the import mode to be robust.
- Ensure the scheduler descriptor for `gh-import` matches the schema (add `agent` field if required, set `frequency_minutes:30`, `max_runtime_minutes:5`, metadata). Add deduplication/locking if appropriate.
- Add or update unit and integration tests covering normal import, rate limits, and failure/retry behavior.
- Update documentation and scheduler_store examples.

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNFSNFUS000TI5B — Task: gh-push scheduler command (worklog). Complementary scheduled task that uses the same run_gh_sync wrapper; tests and descriptor choices there can be reused for gh-import.
- AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD — Parent epic (worklog). Parent epic for refactoring scheduled tasks: gh-import changes should align with the epic's migration plan.
- AM-0MNFSNRND0018OL4 — Task: pr-monitor scheduler command (worklog). Useful for observable scheduling and runtime constraints patterns.
- AM-0MNU7MXP40060TW7 — Automated intake scheduled task (worklog). Shows scheduling and dispatch patterns and the "do not ask questions" intake style used by automated runners.
- src/ampa/run_gh_sync.py (repo file). Primary implementation to inspect for import and push modes; update here for robustness.
- tests/test_run_gh_sync.py (repo file). Unit tests to use as a baseline for regression coverage.
- src/ampa/scheduler_schema.md (repo file). Canonical scheduler schema; ensure descriptor validates against this schema.
- .worklog/logs/sync.log (repo file). Historical sync logs mentioning this work item; useful for tracing previous edits or conflicts.


Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "Should any clarifying questions be asked during this intake?" — Answer (user instruction): "do not ask questions". Source: initial intake command. Final: yes.
- Q: "Who should be assigned to implement the work?" — Answer (agent inference): "Map". Source: work item assignee field on AM-0MNFSNCPY006HJ2W. Final: yes.
- Q: "Is a migration required? (yes/no)" — Answer (agent inference): "No evidence of schema migration required". Source: inspected scheduler schema and related work items. Final: yes.

data.description:
Ensure the scheduler `gh-push` task reliably runs every 3 hours to push local Worklog changes to GitHub, with robust error handling, tests, and updated docs.

Problem statement

Scheduled task `gh-push` currently exists as a scheduler entry but the implementation is broken or incomplete and needs review and re-implementation so that local Worklog changes are reliably pushed to GitHub every 3 hours.

Users

- Operators and maintainers who rely on automated synchronization of local Worklog changes to GitHub.  
- Example user story: "As an operator, I want the scheduler to reliably run the `gh-push` task every 3 hours so that local worklog changes are mirrored to GitHub without manual intervention."

Success criteria

- The `gh-push` command runs every 180 minutes and respects the 5 minute max runtime under normal conditions.  
- When the command succeeds, a `wl github push` operation (or equivalent) is executed and GitHub receives the pushed changes (verified by checking remote branch status or `wl show` evidence).  
- Failures are logged and surfaced via a wl comment on the related work item with structured error text; transient failures are retried according to scheduler retry policy.  
- Tests: unit tests for the `ampa.run_gh_sync` wrapper and an integration test for the scheduler invocation of `gh-push` are present and passing.  
- All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.  
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

Constraints

- The scheduler command must use existing `ampa.run_gh_sync` wrapper module and follow project conventions for invoking shell commands.  
- Change must avoid introducing secrets or writing credentials into repo files.  
- Maintain backward compatibility with existing scheduler config keys (id `gh-push`, frequency 180 minutes, type `shell`) unless explicitly agreed otherwise.

Existing state

- Work item: AM-0MNFSNFUS000TI5B — "Task: gh-push scheduler command" (status: in-progress, stage: idea). Parent: AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD.  
- Code: `src/ampa/run_gh_sync.py` implements the wrapper used by scheduler. Tests exist in `tests/test_run_gh_sync.py`. Scheduler definitions reference the command in `src/ampa/scheduler_schema.md`.  
- README and scheduler schema mention `gh-push` and `ampa.run_gh_sync`. Some scheduler tests and helpers interact with intake/scheduler flows.

Desired change

- Review and, if necessary, re-implement `gh-push` scheduler command to: ensure correct command invocation, robust error handling, clear logging, and adherence to runtime/cadence constraints.  
- Add or update unit tests for `ampa.run_gh_sync` and an integration test to validate the scheduler invokes the command and that the `push` mode performs the expected `wl github push` operation (mocked).  
- Update scheduler schema docs and README entries to reflect any behavior or configuration changes.

Related work

- src/ampa/run_gh_sync.py — wrapper module used by `gh-push`. (path)
- src/ampa/scheduler_schema.md — contains scheduler entry for `gh-push` and schema examples. (path)
- src/ampa/README.md — documents `gh-push` and usage of `ampa.run_gh_sync`. (path)
- tests/test_run_gh_sync.py — existing unit tests for the wrapper. (path)
- AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD — Parent work item grouping scheduler changes. (work item)
- AM-0MNFSNRND0018OL4 — Task: pr-monitor scheduler command (related scheduler tasks). (work item)

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "Are clarifying questions allowed?" — Answer (user instruction): "do not ask questions". Source: seed input. Final: yes.  

Notes

- This draft was created without an interactive interview because the seed instruction explicitly requested no questions. If additional detail is required (for example: exact retry policy, log format expectations, or whether to change command cadence) please provide those specifics in a follow-up.  

Risks & Assumptions

- Risk: Scheduler retries may cause duplicate pushes if wl push is non-idempotent.
- Risk: Network/credential failures will prevent pushes; ensure failures are logged and retried.
- Assumption: `ampa.run_gh_sync` can be used as-is; if not, wrapper changes will be a small follow-up.
- Mitigation: Record follow-up work items for larger refactors to avoid scope creep.

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD — Parent work item grouping scheduler changes. Relevant because `gh-push` is a scheduler task and needs to align with parent scheduling refactors.
- src/ampa/run_gh_sync.py — wrapper module implementing `import` and `push` modes; contains unit tests in `tests/test_run_gh_sync.py`. This is the primary code to review and extend.
- src/ampa/scheduler_schema.md — defines the `gh-push` entry and its configuration. Useful to confirm expected cadence and runtime.

The wl-audit scheduled command in AMPA appears to be non-functional and needs a focused code review to identify bugs, missing behaviors, and to produce a concise pseudo-code representation to guide fixes or reimplementation.

- Operators / maintainers of AMPA who rely on scheduled audits to assess project health.

- As a maintainer, I want a clear pseudo-code representation and a list of defects so I can implement fixes with confidence.

2. All correctness, reliability, and scheduling issues are identified and documented with file:line references where applicable.

4. The work item is updated with the review findings and a recommended next step (fixes or re-implementation plan).

data.description:
Title: pr-monitor scheduler command (AM-0MNFSNRND0018OL4)

Problem statement
The existing pr-monitor scheduled task does not operate reliably and requires review and re-implementation to ensure it monitors pull requests every hour, respects deduplication, and completes within the configured runtime limits.

Users
- Support engineers and maintainers who rely on automated PR monitoring to surface stale or important PRs.
- Release engineers who depend on accurate PR metrics and notifications.

User stories
- As a support engineer, I want the pr-monitor to run every hour and process up to 50 PRs so I can get up-to-date PR status without manual checks.
- As an operator, I want the task to finish within 10 minutes and be safe to run concurrently (dedup: true) so it does not overload the system.

Success criteria
- The pr-monitor runs on a 60-minute schedule and executes reliably on the scheduler (observable via logs). 
- Each run completes within the configured timeout (<= 600 seconds) under normal load for up to max_prs (50). 
- The task respects deduplication semantics: concurrent dispatches are deduplicated and only one active instance runs per schedule window. 
- All functional behavior covered by unit and integration tests; automated tests exercising the scheduler invocation and PR-processing logic pass. 
- All related documentation (README, scheduler docs, code comments, and any relevant wiki pages) is updated to reflect the implementation.

Constraints
- Max runtime: 600 seconds (10 minutes).
- Max PRs per run: 50 (configurable metadata must be respected). 
- Priority and scheduling must integrate with existing scheduler schema and parent work item (AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD). 
- Respect existing metadata fields: dedup: true, container_dispatch_timeout_seconds, max_prs.

Existing state
- Work item AM-0MNFSNRND0018OL4 currently documents a pr-monitor scheduled task (status: in-progress, stage: idea) and references parent AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD. 
- The current implementation has configuration metadata (frequency: 60m, max_prs: 50, dedup: true, container_dispatch_timeout_seconds: 600) but has been marked as needing review and re-implementation.

Desired change
- Review and re-implement the pr-monitor scheduler command to be robust and observable: ensure scheduling, deduplication, runtime limits, and PR-processing logic are correct and covered by tests. Update the scheduler registration and documentation.

Related work
- Parent work item: AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD — parent grouping scheduler changes.
- AM-0MNFT0NHT0051BP9 — Task: delegation and auto-delegate scheduler commands (related scheduler work).
- AM-0MNU7MXP40060TW7 — Automated intake scheduled task (intake automation patterns and dispatch examples).

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNFT0NHT0051BP9: Delegation and auto-delegate scheduler commands — shares scheduler patterns for descriptor fields and runtime constraints that pr-monitor should follow.
- AM-0MNU7MXP40060TW7: Automated intake scheduled task — shows examples of automated dispatch and "do not ask questions" intake patterns which this work item follows.
- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63: Intake query and selection — contains tests and registration examples for auto-registered commands; useful for unit test patterns.
- AM-0MNFSNJ3W00787T5: wl-audit scheduler command — another scheduler command with higher priority; review of its implementation and tests can provide patterns for observability and error handling.

data.description:
Headline: Consolidate delegation report and auto-delegate scheduler commands into a single, testable scheduled task

Problem statement

The repository contains two related scheduler commands (delegation report and auto-delegate) that together manage work item delegation but are implemented separately and the auto-delegate task is currently disabled. This has caused gaps in delegation automation and inconsistent agent assignment behaviour. The work is to consolidate, design, and re-enable a robust delegation/auto-delegate scheduled task with clear acceptance criteria.

Users

- Operators: platform/ops staff who rely on accurate delegation reports to monitor outstanding work.
- Engineers: agents and maintainers who depend on automatic assignment of work items to available agents.
- Product managers: who expect predictable handoff and assignment across the pipeline.

User stories

- As an operator, I want the delegation report to run reliably at its configured cadence and surface accurate assignment metrics so I can triage backlog items.
- As an engineer, I want auto-delegate to assign eligible work items automatically according to config rules so work is routed without manual intervention.
- As a product manager, I want evidence (logs/metrics) that delegation decisions are made and the ability to audit and override assignments.

Success criteria

- Delegation report runs at 2-minute cadence and completes within 5 minutes; produced report matches expected schema and is written to the configured sink (Measurable: cron schedule entry present, logs show successful runs, output schema validated).
- Auto-delegate runs at 30-minute cadence and completes within 5 minutes when enabled; eligible work items are assigned according to policy (Measurable: sample set of eligible items assigned deterministically; acceptance test verifies assignments).
- The auto-delegate task can be toggled (enabled/disabled) by configuration and respects eligible_stages and eligible_priorities filters.
- All related documentation (README, module docstrings, and operator-runbooks) updated to reflect new behaviour.
- Full project test suite passes, and new unit/integration tests cover delegation logic and scheduling.

Constraints

- Must preserve existing parent/child relationships for work items and not change persisted ownership unless rules dictate.
- Respect existing scheduling framework and do not introduce new runtime dependencies unless approved.
- Follow repository coding, testing, and documentation standards.

Existing state

- Two scheduler commands exist: delegation (2-minute, audit/report) and auto-delegate (30-minute, currently disabled). See scheduler definitions and existing tests in src/ampa and scheduler docs.
- Work item AM-0MNFT0NHT0051BP9 exists and is assigned to Map (task type, parent AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD).

Desired change

- Consolidate implementation into a single module or package that exposes both report and auto-delegate behaviours with clear configuration toggles.
- Add deterministic assignment logic respecting eligible_stages and eligible_priorities, with unit and integration tests.
- Add logging, metrics, and operator documentation for observability and manual override instructions.

Related work

- src/ampa/scheduler_schema.md — scheduler schema and existing cron-like definitions
- AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD — Parent work item grouping scheduler changes
- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection (related intake work)
- AM-0MNUB5351000HNC9 — Intake completion detection (observability overlap)

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "Are there existing assignment policies (tie-breakers, agent eligibility) documented?" — Answer (agent inference): No explicit policy found in scheduler docs; existing code contains heuristics in src/ampa/scheduler_selector and tests. Source: inspected src/ampa/* and scheduler docs. Final: agent inference.
- Q: "Should consolidation change the schedule cadence or keep existing frequencies?" — Answer (user/seed): keep existing cadences (2 minutes for report, 30 minutes for auto-delegate). Source: seed work item. Final: keep existing cadences.
- Q: "Is enabling auto-delegate permitted or should it remain disabled until operator signoff?" — Answer (user/seed): enable as part of this work with a configuration flag to toggle; record this decision. Source: seed. Final: enable behind config flag.


Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection: contains scheduling selection logic and prior intake patterns that are used by the scheduler. Relevant because it implements query candidates and selection heuristics used by delegation tasks.
- AM-0MNUB5351000HNC9 — Intake completion detection: contains observability and detection patterns for intake completion; useful for building delegation report metrics.
- AM-0MNUB5AK4000V1V2 — State/Status-based agent assignment: defines deterministic state×stage assignment policies that should be considered when implementing auto-delegate.
- src/ampa/scheduler_schema.md — repository scheduler schema & existing definitions. Important source for schedule formatting and persistence keys.


Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Auto-assignment could assign inappropriate items if eligibility rules are incomplete; mitigation: start behind a feature flag and run in dry-run mode.
- Risk: Scheduling collisions or long-running tasks affecting cadence; mitigation: add max runtime checks and alerts.
- Assumption: Existing scheduler schema and selector code will be reused.

data.description:
# QA Nightly Runner - Scheduled Task

## Problem Statement
The project currently runs QA checks (linting, testing, type checking) only during CI on push/PR events. There is no automated, regular comprehensive quality assessment running outside of active development cycles. We need a nightly scheduled task that performs extensive QA checks to catch regressions, code quality issues, and technical debt accumulation that might be missed during regular CI.

## Users
- **Developers**: Receive notifications about code quality issues, test failures, or regressions discovered overnight
- **Maintainers/Project Leads**: Monitor overall project health trends, coverage metrics, and technical debt accumulation
- **DevOps/Infrastructure**: Ensure the automated QA pipeline runs reliably and integrates with existing notification systems

## User Stories
- As a developer, I want to be notified via Discord if the nightly QA run finds any critical issues so I can address them promptly
- As a maintainer, I want a comprehensive quality report generated nightly so I can track project health trends over time
- As a team lead, I want critical failures to automatically create work items so they don't get lost in notifications
- As a developer, I want code smell detection running regularly so technical debt is caught early

## Success Criteria
1. Nightly runner executes at 4 AM daily without manual intervention
2. Runner performs all standard QA checks: linting (black, isort, flake8, mypy), testing (pytest with coverage), and build verification
3. Extended checks execute: security scanning, code complexity analysis, code smell detection (pylint, bandit, vulture), dependency vulnerability checks
4. Results are summarized and posted to Discord with clear pass/fail status
5. Any failures automatically create critical work items in the worklog
6. Coverage trends and quality metrics are tracked and reported
7. Runner integrates with the AMPA scheduler system as a scheduled task
8. Historical results are persisted for trend analysis

## Constraints
- Must integrate with existing AMPA scheduler architecture (child of AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD)
- Must use existing tooling from pyproject.toml (black, isort, flake8, mypy, pytest)
- Must not duplicate existing CI functionality unnecessarily
- Must be idempotent - running multiple times shouldn't create duplicate work items
- Must handle transient failures gracefully (e.g., network issues during security scans)

## Existing State
- GitHub Actions CI runs on push/PR with lint, test, and build jobs
- Project uses Poetry for dependency management
- Code quality tools configured: black, isort, flake8, mypy
- Test suite uses pytest with coverage reporting
- AMPA scheduler system is being re-architected (parent work item AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD)
- Discord integration exists for notifications
- Worklog system exists for creating/managing work items

## Desired Change
Implement a new scheduled task type "qa-nightly-runner" that:
1. Executes comprehensive QA checks at 4 AM daily
2. Generates structured reports with metrics and findings
3. Posts summary to Discord with relevant details
4. Creates critical work items for any failures or regressions
5. Tracks and reports coverage trends over time
6. Potentially stores results for historical comparison

## Risks & Assumptions
**Risks:**
- **Scope creep**: Extended QA checks (security scanning, code complexity analysis) could expand beyond initial scope. *Mitigation: Document each new check as a separate work item linked to this main item rather than expanding scope.*
- **Scheduler dependency**: This work depends on the scheduler re-architecture (AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD) being completed first
- **Noise from false positives**: Automated code smell detection may generate false positives that create unnecessary work items
- **Resource consumption**: Extended QA checks may be resource-intensive and slow

**Assumptions:**
- AMPA scheduler system will support custom scheduled task types
- Discord integration will be available for notifications
- Worklog API supports programmatic creation of critical work items
- Test suite and tooling configurations in pyproject.toml are up-to-date and working

## Related Work
- **AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD**: "Strip all scheduled tasks, re-add one at a time" (parent epic) - The scheduler re-architecture work this task must integrate with
- **AM-0MNFSNA5B004ULRI**: cleanup-runner scheduler command (high priority) - Similar scheduled task pattern to follow
- **AM-0MNFSNJ3W00787T5**: wl-audit scheduler command (high priority) - Similar audit/reporting pattern
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: Existing CI pipeline with lint/test jobs
- `pyproject.toml`: Tool configurations for black, isort, flake8, mypy, pytest

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers

- **Q1**: "What specific QA tasks should the nightly runner perform?"
  - **Answer (user)**: Everything you can think of should be a candidate at this stage. When we plan implementation we will phase the work and possibly split it out into separate tasks.
  - **Follow-up**: Comprehensive suite including standard CI checks plus extended analysis (security, complexity, code smells, dependency vulnerabilities)

- **Q2**: "When should the nightly runner execute and how should results be handled?"
  - **Answer (user)**: 4am daily, Results will go to various places, e.g. discord notification summary, new issues raised for problems found. Failures should create critical work items.

- **Q3**: "Is this related to the scheduler re-architecture work (AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD)?"
  - **Answer (user)**: Yes
  - **Action**: Set AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD as parent work item

---
*Draft created: Intake process for QA nightly runner*

## Summary

The change updates poll_and_handoff to record a per-item last_attempt_at on every handoff and to update last_audit_at_by_item only when the handler indicates success. Unit tests exercise handler returning False, raising an exception, and returning True and assert persisted state for each case; implementation in src/ampa/audit_poller.py implements the behaviour.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | On handler failure (handler raises or returns False) store.last_audit_at_by_item is not updated (or last_success_at unchanged); last_attempt is recorded if implemented. | met | tests/test_audit_poller.py:71-76 — asserts last_attempt recorded and last_audit not set on failure; src/ampa/audit_poller.py:572-576 — records last_attempt and does not set last_audit when handler fails. |
| 2 | On handler success, last_audit_at_by_item is updated to now.isoformat(). | met | tests/test_audit_poller.py:127-131 — asserts last_audit set on success; src/ampa/audit_poller.py:576-579 — sets last_audit_at_by_item when handler returns truthy. |
| 3 | Tests cover both failure and success paths. | met | tests/test_audit_poller.py:51-132 — unit tests exercise False, exception, and success paths and assert persisted state. |

## Children Status

No children.

data.description:
Problem: The audit poller persists last_audit_at BEFORE calling the handler. If the handler fails, the item is skipped for the cooldown window and retries are suppressed.

Decision: Persist last_audit only when the handler completes successfully (handler returns True). This follows the recommended behaviour to avoid hiding failures. Also record last_attempt_at for observability if useful.

Work: Modify poll_and_handoff to persist only on handler success; add last_attempt_at vs last_success_at if helpful; add integration tests that simulate handler failure and success.

Acceptance criteria:
- On handler failure (handler raises or returns False) store.last_audit_at_by_item is not updated (or last_success_at unchanged); last_attempt is recorded if implemented.
- On handler success, last_audit_at_by_item is updated to now.isoformat().
- Tests cover both failure and success paths.

Notes: This is the recommended semantics per intake.
data.title: Fix: audit_poller persist last_audit only on handler success

data.description:
Problem: _query_candidates deduplicates by id using last-wins semantics. That can keep a newer representation and change ordering semantics.

Work: When deduplicating by work item id, prefer the representation with the older updated_at (older = earlier timestamp) or otherwise merge fields deterministically. Update unit tests to assert deterministic behaviour.

Acceptance criteria:
- Dedup preserves the older item representation when duplicate ids are present.
- Unit tests added verifying duplicate inputs with different updatedAt values result in the older version being retained.

Related: audit_poller._query_candidates, _item_updated_ts
data.title: Fix: audit_poller dedup logic — prefer older updated_at

- poll_and_handoff returns PollerOutcome.query_failed when wl returns non-zero rc or invalid JSON.

- A unit/integration test simulates missing descriptor and asserts the scheduler/audit flow logs the expected error.

data.description:
# Test _from_iso

Problem statement

The repository contains multiple implementations of a utility named `_from_iso` used to parse ISO-8601 timestamps. This intake captures the tests required to verify expected semantics (accept trailing `Z`, parse offsets, coerce naive datetimes to UTC) and the minimal follow-up steps for potential consolidation.

Users

- Library maintainers
- Test authors and CI engineers
- Callers of `_from_iso` across scheduler, audit, delegation, and CLI modules

Success criteria

- Tests exist that assert the required behaviours: parse trailing `Z` as UTC, accept explicit offsets, and coerce naive ISO strings to UTC.
- All existing callers continue to behave identically when tests run.
- No behavioural regressions introduced.

Constraints

- Prefer minimal, non-breaking changes. Avoid large refactors in the intake stage.
- Keep tests deterministic and timezone-aware (use explicit tz-aware datetimes in assertions).
- Do not change public behaviour unless explicitly approved.

Existing state

- `_from_iso` implementations present in at least two modules:
  - `src/ampa/audit_poller.py` defines `_from_iso` (coerces trailing `Z`, offsets, and naive -> UTC).
  - `src/ampa/scheduler_types.py` defines `_from_iso` with the same behaviour.
- There are existing tests in `tests/test_audit_poller.py` covering:
  - trailing `Z` terminator
  - `+00:00` offset
  - naive datetime assumed as UTC
- A recent commit message in the git logs references coercing naive ISO datetimes to UTC for `_from_iso`, indicating the behaviour was explicitly implemented.

Desired change

- Create or formalise unit tests that assert the three behaviours listed above against a single canonical implementation (or against both modules if we choose to keep two copies while deciding consolidation).
- Prefer referencing the implementation in `scheduler_types.py` from other modules where appropriate to avoid duplication, but only after tests validate behaviour and a short review.

Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Scope creep — consolidating implementations could touch many callers. Mitigation: perform consolidation in a follow-up work item and only after tests pass; record additional refactor opportunities as separate work items.
- Risk: Hidden input variants — callers may pass non-string datetime-like objects. Assumption: for this intake we only verify string ISO inputs; handling additional types is a follow-up.
- Risk: Timezone edge cases (leap seconds, uncommon offsets) may behave differently across python versions. Mitigation: keep tests focused on common ISO variants and document unexpected behaviour as a follow-up.

Related work

- `tests/test_audit_poller.py` — current tests exercising `_from_iso` in audit poller context.
- Recent git activity: commit referencing `Coerce naive ISO datetimes to UTC in _from_iso` (seen in local git logs).
- Parent work item: `Task: wl-audit scheduler command (AM-0MNFSNJ3W00787T5)` — this work item is an ancestor; this test task is a child.
- Implementation locations:
  - `src/ampa/audit_poller.py` (defines `_from_iso`)
  - `src/ampa/scheduler_types.py` (defines `_from_iso`)

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNG2ZIEC005TVK9 — Fix: audit_poller timezone parsing (coerce to UTC)
  This completed task updated `audit_poller._from_iso` to coerce naive ISO strings to UTC and added unit tests covering `Z`, `+00:00`, and naive inputs. It directly overlaps the test cases you plan to add and may already contain the assertions or helper test fixtures you need.

- AM-0MNG2ZIIE001EOUQ — Fix: audit_poller persist last_audit only on handler success
  This completed change adjusts when the poller persists audit timestamps (recording `last_audit` only on handler success and using `last_attempt` for observability). It's relevant for tests that examine store timestamps or cooldown behaviour following handler outcomes.

- AM-0MNG2ZIW80098HEO — Implement tests: consolidate and add CI entries for audit_poller tests
  This open task concerns adding/organising audit_poller tests and CI configuration. It may influence where to place tests and whether CI already runs the new test files.

Repository pointers (repo files relevant to this work):
- `src/ampa/audit_poller.py` — contains the implementation of `_from_iso` and callers; review lines around the `_from_iso` definition and its uses.
- `tests/test_audit_poller.py` — existing tests that already exercise `_from_iso` variants; review to avoid duplicating assertions.
- `tests/test_audit_poller_additional.py` — other `audit_poller` tests exercising `poll_and_handoff` and related behaviour.

Notes / next steps (automated):
1) Review AM-0MNG2ZIEC005TVK9 and the commit referenced there to see if the tests you intend to add already exist or were merged; if so, your test work item may be redundant or should be adjusted to add any missing cases.
2) If your tests assert store persistence or cooldown behaviour, consider the semantics introduced by AM-0MNG2ZIIE001EOUQ when writing expectations (last_attempt vs last_audit semantics).

Appendix — Clarifying Q&A

Q: Is `_from_iso` behaviour already matching requested tests?
A: Yes — both implementations accept trailing `Z`, parse explicit offsets, and coerce naive datetimes to UTC. The existing tests in `tests/test_audit_poller.py` already cover these cases.

Q: Should we immediate consolidate duplicate implementations?
A: Recommendation: add/verify tests first. If tests pass and behaviour is stable, consolidate to a single implementation (prefer `scheduler_types.py`) in a follow-up work item to avoid accidental divergence.

Q: What is the minimal first change to satisfy this intake?
A: Add/confirm unit tests that assert the three behaviours against the canonical function, run CI. Do not change code in this intake.

---

Headline (1–2 sentence summary): Add unit tests validating `_from_iso` ISO-8601 parsing semantics (accept trailing `Z`, accept `+00:00` offsets, and coerce naive datetimes to UTC); run under CI. If tests pass, consider consolidating duplicate implementations in a follow-up task.

data.description:
Problem statement

Unit tests are needed for the internal utility `_query_candidates` to verify it correctly parses the variety of shapes returned by upstream providers (bare lists, wrapped dicts with keys like `workItems`, `work_items`, `items`, `data`, and keys ending with `workitems`) and to validate deduplication semantics when duplicate ids appear with differing `updatedAt` timestamps.

Headline

Add unit tests to verify `_query_candidates` normalises multiple response shapes and enforces a clear deduplication rule for duplicate ids (prefer newer `updatedAt`).

Users

- Library maintainers and test authors who will rely on deterministic parsing behaviour.
- Callers in `audit_poller`, `scheduler`, and `delegation` modules that consume `_query_candidates` outputs.

User stories

- As a maintainer, I want tests that assert `_query_candidates` correctly normalises different response shapes so regressions are caught by CI.
- As a caller (audit_poller, scheduler, delegation), I want consistent deduplication so callers always receive a single canonical record per id.

Success criteria

1. Tests exist that assert `_query_candidates` parses: bare lists, wrapped dicts (workItems, work_items, items, data), and keys ending with `workitems`.
2. Tests verify deduplication semantics: when duplicate ids appear with different `updatedAt`, the utility keeps the newer record (highest `updatedAt`).
3. Production code updated so `_query_candidates` prefers the newer `updatedAt` when deduplicating; change is covered by tests.
4. Tests and implementation changes are committed in a PR, CI green, and reviewed.
5. Tests include explicit assertions for edge cases such as empty responses, unexpected keys, and mixed-type lists so failures are easy to diagnose.

Constraints

- Prefer minimal, targeted production changes to update dedup behaviour; avoid broad refactors in this work item.
- Tests must be deterministic and not rely on network access; use fixtures or mocks.

Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Existing code currently prefers older `updatedAt` when deduping; changing behaviour may conflict with prior fixes or expectations. Mitigation: coordinate with maintainers, add changelog note, and include regression tests; consider creating a follow-up item if broader changes are required.
- Risk: Non-deterministic tests due to timezone/timestamp parsing. Mitigation: use fixed UTC timestamps and reuse `_from_iso` fixtures.
- Assumption: `_query_candidates` is the canonical place to enforce dedup semantics; if other callers duplicate this logic, they will be updated or tested accordingly.

Existing state

- `_query_candidates` is implemented in `src/ampa/audit_poller.py`; current implementation prefers the older `updatedAt` when deduplicating (see code comments and logic in the function).
- There are existing tests in `tests/test_audit_poller.py` and `tests/test_audit_poller_additional.py` that exercise related behaviour and contain fixtures that can be reused.

Desired change

- Update the `_query_candidates` deduplication logic to prefer the row with the newer `updatedAt` timestamp when duplicate ids are present.
- Add unit tests (in `tests/test_audit_poller.py`) that cover parsing shapes and dedup behaviour, including edge cases and fixtures.

Related work

- AM-0MNFSNJ3W00787T5 — Review wl-audit scheduler command (parent epic; references audit_poller)
- AM-0MNG2ZIK6005P27M — Fix: audit_poller dedup logic — prefer older updated_at (prior fix; review for conflict)
- AM-0MNG2ZILA006V7AX — Fix: audit_poller JSON parsing robustness & invalid JSON handling
- AM-0MNG2ZIEC005TVK9 — Fix: audit_poller timezone parsing (coerce to UTC)
- AM-0MNG2ZINZ007P6ZI — Test: _from_iso timezone parsing (Z, +00:00, naive)
- files: `src/ampa/audit_poller.py`, `tests/test_audit_poller.py`, `tests/test_audit_poller_additional.py`

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "Which caller modules and code paths should these _query_candidates tests cover (e.g., audit_poller, scheduler, delegation, other)? Please select the most relevant?" — Answer (user): "all of the above". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "When duplicate ids appear with different updatedAt values, which record should _query_candidates keep?" — Answer (user): "Keep the newer record (higher updatedAt)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "Where should the tests be added?" — Answer (user): "tests/test_audit_poller.py (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "Which behaviour should be considered authoritative: keep current code (older) or update code to prefer newer?" — Answer (user): "A — update code to prefer newer (higher updatedAt)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes. Research: inspected `src/ampa/audit_poller.py` and confirmed the current implementation prefers older `updatedAt` when deduplicating (see the dedup logic and _item_updated_ts usage). Change required: update logic to prefer newer timestamps and add tests that validate the new behaviour.
data.title: Test: _query_candidates parsing shapes & dedup behavior

data.description:
Reproduction

1. Run: wl ampa start-work "OB-0MNGP56HU0026T1I"
2. Observe ReferenceError thrown by ampa plugin

Observed error

file:///home/rgardler/.config/opencode/.worklog/plugins/ampa.mjs:1794
    `rm -rf "${CONTAINER_PROJECT_ROOT}" || true`,
               ^

ReferenceError: CONTAINER_PROJECT_ROOT is not defined
    at startWork (file:///home/rgardler/.config/opencode/.worklog/plugins/ampa.mjs:1794:16)
    at Command.<anonymous> (file:///home/rgardler/.config/opencode/.worklog/plugins/ampa.mjs:2589:26)
    at Command.listener [as _actionHandler] (/home/rgardler/projects/ContextHub/node_modules/commander/lib/command.js:494:17)

Context

- Node.js v22.22.0
- Plugin path: ~/.config/opencode/.worklog/plugins/ampa.mjs

Likely cause

The startWork implementation references CONTAINER_PROJECT_ROOT but that variable is not defined in the function scope. This causes a ReferenceError when the template literal is evaluated. The variable name may be misspelled or not initialized in certain code paths.

Suggested fix

- Ensure the correct variable is referenced (declare or derive CONTAINER_PROJECT_ROOT in startWork), or use an existing variable name for the container project path.
- Add defensive checks before using the variable so start-work fails with a clear error message instead of throwing.
- Add tests covering the start-work flow to prevent regressions.

Acceptance criteria

- Running `wl ampa start-work "<work-item-id>"` no longer throws ReferenceError for CONTAINER_PROJECT_ROOT.
- The sandbox container creation proceeds as expected for the same input (pre-warmed container path is used).
- A unit or integration test is added to cover this code path and prevent regressions.
data.title: Critical: Fix ReferenceError in ampa plugin start-work (CONTAINER_PROJECT_ROOT not defined)

data.description:
Problem statement

Running `wl ampa start-work "<work-item-id>"` can throw a ReferenceError because the variable `CONTAINER_PROJECT_ROOT` is referenced by the startWork implementation but not defined in certain code paths. This causes the command to fail with an uncaught exception instead of returning a clear, actionable error.

Headline

Fix ReferenceError in ampa start-work: ensure CONTAINER_PROJECT_ROOT is defined and start-work returns clear errors when prerequisites are missing.

Users

- Plugin users and maintainers who run `wl ampa start-work <id>` to claim or prepare a dev container for working on a work item.
- Automation and CI that rely on start-work for pre-warmed container workflows.

User stories

- As a developer using the ampa plugin, I want `wl ampa start-work <id>` to reliably prepare a working dev container or fail with a clear error message so I can start work without debugging a ReferenceError.
- As a maintainer, I want tests that cover the start-work container setup path so regressions are detected early.

Success criteria

1. Running `wl ampa start-work "<work-item-id>"` no longer throws ReferenceError for `CONTAINER_PROJECT_ROOT`.
2. The command either claims a pre-warmed container and prepares the repository, or fails with a clear and actionable error message when prerequisites are missing.
3. A unit or integration test exercises the start-work path (happy path and the failing path) and passes in CI.
4. The change is documented in `skill/install-ampa/resources/ampa.mjs` or related README where appropriate.

Constraints

- Changes should be minimal and defensive: prefer adding a variable initialization or defensive check rather than a large refactor unless required.
- Preserve existing behaviour for normal (working) environments; do not break global/local plugin resolution logic.
- Tests should run in the existing Node.js test harness (node test suite), and any new test that depends on container binaries should be marked as manual or skipped in CI if environment lacks deps.

Existing state

- The canonical implementation is `skill/install-ampa/resources/ampa.mjs` (also deployed to `~/.config/opencode/.worklog/plugins/ampa.mjs` when installed). The startWork function and related container helpers are implemented in that file.
- The current work item AM-0MNGPMB6Z009ONJF records the observed ReferenceError and suggested fix.
- There are existing node tests under `tests/node` that exercise parts of the ampa plugin; manual e2e scripts exist for devcontainer lifecycle tests (`tests/manual/test-devcontainer-e2e.sh`).

Desired change

- Identify the correct variable or derive `CONTAINER_PROJECT_ROOT` within the startWork implementation before it is used (e.g., compute from `cName`, `projectRoot`, or existing `CONTAINER_*` helpers).
- Add defensive checks and explicit error messages when required inputs are missing.
- Add or update unit/integration tests to cover the start-work success and failure paths.
- Update any relevant docs describing start-work usage or environment prerequisites.

Related work

- `AM-0MNGPMB6Z009ONJF` — this work item (bug report describing the ReferenceError).
- `skill/install-ampa/resources/ampa.mjs` — canonical plugin implementation (lines around startWork and container helpers).
- `skill/install-ampa/scripts/install-worklog-plugin.sh` — installer script that copies resources into .worklog/plugins/ampa.mjs.
- `tests/node/test-ampa-devcontainer.mjs` and `tests/manual/test-devcontainer-e2e.sh` — existing tests and manual e2e script for container lifecycle.

Potentially related docs

- skill/install-ampa/resources/ampa.mjs (function: startWork, container helpers)
- src/ampa/docs/ampa_container_pool.md (explain devcontainer pool and start-work behaviour)
- tests/manual/test-devcontainer-e2e.sh (manual devcontainer e2e)

Potentially related work items

- AM-0MNGPMB6Z009ONJF — this bug report (current)

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNFSNJ3W00787T5 — Task: wl-audit scheduler command. Related because it exercises AMPA scheduler and container dispatching; useful for understanding how scheduled tasks claim containers and how errors are reported.
- skill/install-ampa/resources/ampa.mjs — canonical implementation of start-work and container pool helpers; contains the exact site of the observed call (`CONTAINER_PROJECT_ROOT` is set/used near the clone/prepare logic).
- src/ampa/docs/ampa_container_pool.md — operational documentation for the devcontainer pool and start-work behaviour; useful for reproducing and validating expected runtime prerequisites (podman/distrobox, pool state).
- tests/node/test-ampa-devcontainer.mjs and tests/manual/test-devcontainer-e2e.sh — existing tests and manual e2e script for devcontainer lifecycle that should be extended or adapted to cover the regression and smoke tests.

This report was generated conservatively by searching for references to start-work, pool containers, and CONTAINER_PROJECT_ROOT in the repository and Worklog. It aims to help developers locate the canonical implementation, reproduce the failure, and add targeted tests.

Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "When does the ReferenceError occur (fresh install, local override, global install)?" — Answer (agent inference): The reported stacktrace points to the installed plugin at `~/.config/opencode/.worklog/plugins/ampa.mjs`; the canonical source is `skill/install-ampa/resources/ampa.mjs`. No user-provided additional context was supplied. Final: agent inference based on the stack trace.
- Q: "Are container runtime binaries (podman/distrobox) expected to be present for tests?" — Answer (user/system): Tests currently include a manual e2e script; new tests that require binaries should be marked manual or skipped in CI. Source: repo tests and docs. Final: yes, mark env-dependent tests as manual/skip in CI.

Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Adding a defensive initialization in one code path may not cover all code paths that need the derived path. Mitigation: add unit tests and a minimal integration/smoke test for the start-work flow.
- Risk: Tests that require container runtimes will be flaky or fail in CI. Mitigation: mark such tests as manual or skip by default in CI; add a CI job with required runtimes if desired later.
- Assumption: The canonical source in `skill/install-ampa/resources/ampa.mjs` is authoritative and changes will be deployed to the installed plugin path via the existing installer script.
- Scope creep mitigation: record additional feature requests (e.g., container pool refactor, enhanced lifecycle metrics) as separate work items linked to this bug rather than expanding this fix. For example: create follow-up tasks for refactor or telemetry if identified.

data.description:
Headline

Finalize an intake brief for AM-0MNK8POBA001SN11 (requested: "do not ask questions") — produce a concise problem statement, measurable acceptance criteria, constraints, and related work so implementation can proceed or missing information is recorded as OPEN QUESTIONS.

Problem statement

The seed intent attached to work item AM-0MNK8POBA001SN11 was submitted with the directive "do not ask questions" and a short title: "Add a scheduled command to run every night at 11pm that runs all tests". The request lacks implementation details and acceptance criteria. This intake documents existing state, records open questions (per the directive), and provides measurable success criteria to allow downstream planning and implementation.

Users

- Primary: Internal engineering/ops teams responsible for test automation and maintenance; secondary: repository maintainers and release engineers.
- Example user stories:
  - As an operator, I want a scheduled job that runs the full test suite nightly so I can detect regressions early.
  - As an engineer, I want failures to produce actionable work items automatically so triage time is reduced.


Success criteria

- SC1: The work item includes a clear, implementable description and at least 3 measurable acceptance criteria (measured: description contains an "Acceptance Criteria" section with >=3 bullets).
- SC2: Nightly scheduled command executes at 23:00 local/UTC as defined by deploy/config (measured: scheduler logs show job ran at 23:00 for 3 consecutive nights in staging).
- SC3: On failure, the system creates or links to an existing critical bug work item with test outputs and relevant logs (measured: automatic work item created with failure artifacts attached in >=90% of test failures during a trial run).
- SC4: All related documentation is updated to reflect the change, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- SC5: Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.


Constraints

- Preserve the "do not ask questions" directive: do not contact the requester; record missing information as OPEN QUESTIONS in the Appendix.
- Respect existing scheduler and worklog conventions (do not modify scheduler_store.json format without explicit review).
- Keep scope limited to scheduling, detection, and work-item creation; do not expand into large-scale CI infra changes without separate work items.


Existing state

- Work item AM-0MNK8POBA001SN11 currently exists with title: "Add a scheduled command to run every night at 11pm that runs all tests" and is assigned to Map at stage=idea status=in-progress.
- Scheduler store contains an entry for AM-0MNK8POBA001SN11 in .worklog/ampa/scheduler_store.json (potential scheduled command candidate).
- No detailed acceptance criteria, schedule timezone, targeted environment (staging vs production), or failure-handling rules were provided in the work item description.


Desired change

- Implement a scheduled command that, once deployed, runs the full test suite nightly at 23:00 and creates or updates a critical work item for any failures with attached diagnostics. Provide configuration options for target environment and timezone, and a toggle to enable/disable automatic issue creation.


Related work

- Potentially related docs (file paths):
  - .worklog/ampa/scheduler_store.json — scheduler store referencing scheduled commands and timestamps.
  - .worklog/logs/sync.log — historical intake and conflict records mentioning scheduled commands and the work item id.
  - .opencode/tmp/intake-draft-do-not-ask-questions-AM-0MNK8POBA001SN11.md — this draft intake (this file).

- Potentially related work items:
  - Add a scheduled command to run every night at 11pm that runs all tests — AM-0MNK8POBA001SN11
  - Intake: W1 (do not ask questions) — AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU (appears in logs as a similar non-interactive intake)

Summaries

- .worklog/ampa/scheduler_store.json: contains recorded scheduled commands and a timestamp entry for AM-0MNK8POBA001SN11. Source of truth for currently configured scheduled tasks.
- .worklog/logs/sync.log: multiple entries referencing conflicts and intake runs for work items titled "do not ask questions" and for AM-0MNK8POBA001SN11; useful to trace prior intake attempts and decisions.
- AM-0MNK8POBA001SN11: existing work item titled to add a nightly scheduled test run; description references automatic creation of critical bugs when failures occur, but lacks configuration details (timezone, environment, retention, artifact collection specifics).

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNFT4B0A003278C — QA nightly runner - scheduled task: Directly aligned with this item; defines a nightly QA/run tests job, reporting, Discord notifications, and automatic creation of critical work items on failures. Use as the primary implementation template and to capture expected checks, tooling and notification behaviour.

- AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD — Strip all scheduled tasks, re-add one at a time: Parent epic for scheduler re-architecture and safe rollout of scheduled commands. Coordinate with this epic to ensure the new nightly-tests command is registered and deployed in the intended scheduler migration order.

- AM-0MNFSNJ3W00787T5 — Task: wl-audit scheduler command: Provides a concrete pattern for a scheduled job that runs audits, detects failures, and creates follow-up work items. Inspect its failure-detection, logging, and notification behaviour when implementing test-run failure handling.

- AM-0MNK8VAT4004SZBS — Ensure audit is working as expected: Ongoing intake investigating issues with scheduled audit runs. Review its findings to avoid duplicating diagnostics and to adopt any fixes that improve reliability of scheduled runs.

- AM-0MNU6NIEI0055GAA — Make all tests pass: Repository-level work on test stability. The nightly test runner should reference this work for known failing or flaky tests and avoid creating duplicate bug noise for already-tracked test failures.

- AM-0MNU7MXP40060TW7 — Automated intake scheduled task: Collection of scheduler/dispatch/monitoring patterns (query→dispatch→detect→assign) that are useful for running and tracking scheduled commands and their outcomes; useful guidance for selecting, dispatching and recording results of nightly test runs.

(End of automated report)

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- OPEN QUESTION - Q: "What specific change or feature is requested for AM-0MNK8POBA001SN11?" — Answer: NONE PROVIDED (requestor directive: "do not ask questions"). Final: OPEN QUESTION.
- OPEN QUESTION - Q: "Which timezone and environment should the nightly job run in (UTC/staging/production)?" — Answer: NONE PROVIDED. Final: OPEN QUESTION.
- OPEN QUESTION - Q: "On failure, should the system always create a new critical bug, or attempt to link to an existing related work item?" — Answer: The work item text suggests linking to existing items where possible but lacks rules. Final: OPEN QUESTION.
- OPEN QUESTION - Q: "Who is the primary user or stakeholder for this change?" — Answer: NONE PROVIDED. Final: OPEN QUESTION.
- OPEN QUESTION - Q: "Are there any technical constraints, backwards-compatibility requirements, or acceptance tests already expected?" — Answer: NONE PROVIDED. Final: OPEN QUESTION.

Notes

- This draft intentionally avoids inventing requirements beyond minimal, reasonable defaults. All OPEN QUESTIONS are recorded in the Appendix per the directive.

data.description:
Problem statement
The wl-audit scheduled command is producing validation and state-handling errors during daemon runs (see /home/rgardler/projects/ampa/.worklog/ampa/default/default.log). This intake will gather concrete logs, reproduce the failure, and recommend a targeted fix with tests.

Users
- Operators and maintainers who run AMPA and Worklog scheduling (support engineers).
- Developers responsible for the audit poller and scheduler components.

User stories
- As an operator, I want the wl-audit scheduled command to reliably audit in_review work items so I can trust automated audits.
- As a developer, I want reproducible logs and a clear failure reproduction path so I can implement a fix and add tests.

Success criteria
- Gather and attach the relevant log excerpts from /home/rgardler/projects/ampa/.worklog/ampa/default/default.log that show the failure modes (descriptor validation failures and invalid_from_state audit_result errors).
- Produce a minimal reproduction script or steps that reproduce the failure locally.
- Identify the root cause (e.g., workflow descriptor schema mismatch, incorrect work item states, or poller logic bug) and list one or more actionable fixes.
- Provide a patch or clear implementation plan for the highest-confidence fix (or create a follow-up work item to implement it).
- All changes must include tests that reproduce the failure and validate the fix; project test suite passes locally after the fix.

Constraints
- Must not modify production data. Work must be reproducible in local or CI environments.
- Respect Worklog state machine semantics: do not propose changes that break existing stage transitions unless explicitly required and approved.
- Avoid large refactors; prefer small targeted fixes where possible.
- Access to daemon log file path and runtime environment is required; if not available, the investigator must request access or a sanitized copy of logs.

Existing state
- Daemon log (/home/rgardler/projects/ampa/.worklog/ampa/default/default.log) shows repeated errors:
  - "Workflow descriptor validation failed for /.../workflow.yaml: Workflow descriptor validation failed with 1 error(s)"
  - "Audit poller: selected candidate <work-item-id> — <title>"
  - "WARNING audit_result failed for <work-item-id>: invalid_from_state — Work item is in (completed, in_review), expected one of: (in_progress, in_review)"
- There are existing work items related to audit_poller fixes and tests (see Related work below).

Desired change
- Run targeted investigation to collect log excerpts and reproduce the failure.
- If issue is in descriptor validation, correct the workflow descriptor or the validation usage and add tests.
- If issue is in audit_result handling and state transitions, adjust the poller or audit handler to correctly handle item states and add tests.

Risks & assumptions
- Risk: Investigation could expose sensitive log content. Mitigation: request a sanitized log or access via a secure channel.
- Risk: Multiple related issues (descriptor validation, state handling) could be conflated, increasing scope. Mitigation: separate diagnosis steps and create distinct follow-up work items for fixes.
- Assumption: The daemon log path provided is current and contains the failing runs. If not, investigator should confirm the correct log location.
- Assumption: Developers have local environment parity with the daemon runtime (same Python/venv and plugin paths) to reproduce failures.

Related work
- AM-0MNG2ZIIE001EOUQ — Fix: audit_poller persist last_audit only on handler success (completed) — related to persistence semantics observed in logs.
- AM-0MNG2ZIEC005TVK9 — Fix: audit_poller timezone parsing (coerce to UTC) (completed) — background changes to poller time handling.
- AM-0MNG2ZINZ007P6ZI — Test: _from_iso timezone parsing (completed) — related tests.
- AM-0MNFSNJ3W00787T5 — Task: wl-audit scheduler command (open) — may overlap with scheduling behavior.

Potentially related docs
- /home/rgardler/projects/ampa/.worklog/ampa/default/default.log — primary evidence file; contains repeated descriptor validation and audit_result warnings.
- /home/rgardler/.config/opencode/.worklog/plugins/ampa_py/ampa/docs/workflow/workflow.yaml — workflow descriptor referenced in the log validation failures (inspect for schema mismatches).

Related work (automated report)
- AM-0MNG2ZIIE001EOUQ — Fix: audit_poller persist last_audit only on handler success (completed)
  Relevance: Addresses persistence semantics that can cause repeated skips after handler failures; relevant because logs show audit_result state errors and previous fixes changed when last_audit is updated.
- AM-0MNG2ZIEC005TVK9 — Fix: audit_poller timezone parsing (completed)
  Relevance: Historical fixes to datetime parsing in the poller; useful context if descriptor errors are time related or if candidate selection depends on timestamps.
- AM-0MNG2ZINZ007P6ZI — Test: _from_iso timezone parsing (completed)
  Relevance: Tests that validate ISO parsing semantics used by the poller; can be used as a template for reproduction tests.
- AM-0MNFSNJ3W00787T5 — Task: wl-audit scheduler command (open)
  Relevance: Existing intake to review and repair the wl-audit scheduled command; scope overlaps with this investigation and may be the correct parent item for implementation work.



Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
- Q: "Do any existing work items represent the work to gather evidence from the daemon default.log?" — Answer (user): "Use existing: Ensure audit is working as expected (AM-0MNK8VAT4004SZBS) (Recommended)". Source: interactive selection. Final: yes.

Intake summary
Gather logs and reproduce wl-audit failures reported in the daemon log, identify root cause(s), and propose a tested fix or follow-up implementation work item.

## Summary
The implementation adds an `agent` field, executor resolver, Casey default, docs, and several unit tests, but descriptor-validation tests and the required missing-agent validation behaviour are not implemented. Because at least one parent acceptance criterion (CI/tests verification) and the Command Descriptor/Descriptor Validation children have unmet criteria, the parent work item cannot be closed.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | The scheduler accepts and parses an 'agent' field on command descriptors; unit tests validate parsing. | met | src/ampa/scheduler_types.py:100-128 — `CommandSpec` defines `agent` and from_dict/to_dict preserve it. |
| 2 | The executor resolves agent identifiers to endpoint URLs via a configurable mapping and passes the resolved endpoint into default_llm_probe. | met | src/ampa/scheduler_executor.py:137-150 — `resolve_llm_probe_target` returns (agent, endpoint) using `llm_agent_endpoints`. |
| 3 | If no 'agent' is provided in a descriptor, the system resolves to the default agent "Casey". | met | src/ampa/scheduler.py:287-296 — `_effective_llm_agent` falls back to configured/default `Casey`; tests/test_scheduler_scoring.py:189-206 exercise Casey default. |
| 4 | Documentation is added describing the agent field, example mappings (including Casey), and verification steps. | met | docs/scheduler-local-llm.md:10-49 — descriptor example, mapping sample, and verification checklist present. |

| 6 | All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries. | met | docs/scheduler-local-llm.md and src/ampa/README.md updated per commits and child audits. |

## Children Status

### Command Descriptor Agent Field (AM-0MNS4V1DB004SNU9) — in-progress/in_review

| 1 | Descriptor schema accepts an 'agent' or 'model_agent' property and parser extracts it. | met | src/ampa/scheduler_types.py:100-128 — `CommandSpec` defines `agent`; to_dict()/from_dict() include it. |
| 2 | If a descriptor lacks an 'agent' the scheduler will treat it as an error and surface a clear validation failure during enqueue in debug/local-only mode. | unmet | src/ampa/scheduler.py:287-294 — `_effective_llm_agent` and resolver default to "Casey" and do not raise a validation error. |

data.description:
Problem statement

Ensure the AMPA scheduler can resolve an explicit 'agent' identifier in command descriptors to a configured agent endpoint and fall back to a default agent "Casey" when none is provided. This enforces the correct agent selection for scheduled commands and provides a clear default to maintain predictable behavior.

Users

- Operators and on-call engineers who run or inspect scheduled commands.
- Developers writing command descriptors and debugging scheduler-driven flows.

User stories

- As an operator, I want scheduled commands to resolve to a named agent so I can control which agent implementation handles a command.
- As a developer, I want a sensible default (Casey) when a descriptor omits an agent so existing descriptors continue to work without changes.

Success criteria

1. The scheduler accepts and parses an 'agent' field on command descriptors; unit tests validate parsing. (Measurable: tests added)
2. The executor resolves agent identifiers to endpoint URLs via a configurable mapping and passes the resolved endpoint into default_llm_probe. (Measurable: tests assert probe called with resolved endpoint)
3. If no 'agent' is provided in a descriptor, the system resolves to the default agent "Casey". (Measurable: tests and integration checks use Casey as fallback)
4. Documentation is added describing the agent field, example mappings (including Casey), and verification steps. (Measurable: docs file committed)
5. Full project test suite passes with the new changes. (Measurable: CI/local test run)
6. All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.

Constraints

- Preserve current scheduler behaviour unless an agent field is explicitly used or debug/local-only mode requires validation.
- Keep the mapping simple (YAML/JSON in repo or config) and avoid introducing secrets.

Existing state

- Work item AM-0MNO8G0NH00956CY exists and is focused on local-only LLM selection; several children exist for descriptor, executor, tests, logging, and docs.
- No existing agent mapping or "Casey" definition was found in the repository.

Desired change

- Add: descriptor schema 'agent' field and parser changes (child: Command Descriptor Agent Field).
- Add: executor resolver that maps agent -> endpoint and passes agent to llm_probe (child: Executor Agent Resolution).
- Add: a new child work item that creates the Casey agent definition and the example mapping entry.
- Add tests for parsing, resolution, and Casey fallback (Descriptor Validation & CI Tests).
- Add docs and verification steps (Admin Docs & Verification).

Related work

- Command Descriptor Agent Field (AM-0MNS4V1DB004SNU9) — add 'agent' to descriptor schema.
- Executor Agent Resolution (AM-0MNS4V1IJ002SBO7) — resolve agent identifier to endpoint.
- Descriptor Validation & CI Tests (AM-0MNS4V1NS005B3MP) — tests for descriptor validation.
- Logging & Deviation Alerts (AM-0MNS4V1T6009CK3R) — warn on non-local endpoints.
- Admin Docs & Verification (AM-0MNS4V1YF0081FPI) — docs and verification steps.

Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Scope creep; mitigation: record additional feature ideas as separate work items and keep this item focused on agent resolution and Casey default.
- Assumption: No secrets or external credentials are required to define agent mappings in repo config.
- Risk: Changing executor/llm_probe interfaces may require small adaptations in other modules; mitigate by introducing optional parameters and backward-compatible defaults.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "Should Casey be created as a minimal, local-only agent mapping (no external credentials) or should it include a fuller config?"
  - Answer (agent inference): Create Casey as a minimal, local-only mapping that resolves to a project-local endpoint (e.g., http://localhost:PORT or unix socket) and does not include external credentials. Source: repo search found no agent mappings; default should be local. Final: yes.



Related work (automated report)

- Command Descriptor Agent Field (AM-0MNS4V1DB004SNU9): Adds the 'agent' property to descriptors; foundational to resolving agent names.
- Executor Agent Resolution (AM-0MNS4V1IJ002SBO7): Implements mapping from agent identifier to endpoint; necessary for probe/resolution.
- Descriptor Validation & CI Tests (AM-0MNS4V1NS005B3MP): Adds tests ensuring descriptors validate when agent is required.
- Logging & Deviation Alerts (AM-0MNS4V1T6009CK3R): Adds warnings when a non-local endpoint is used; useful for operators verifying Casey fallback.
- Admin Docs & Verification (AM-0MNS4V1YF0081FPI): Documentation and verification steps for operators.
- Scheduler implementation locations: src/ampa/scheduler.py, src/ampa/scheduler_executor.py — primary code to change.
data.title: Scheduler: add 'agent' field and default Casey agent

## Summary

Descriptor lookup precedence is implemented in both engine initialization and scheduler audit execution, and it matches the requested runtime order (env override, project, XDG, package docs). The installer does publish the canonical descriptor into `.worklog/ampa/workflow.json` when running in local install mode and the destination is absent. However, global install mode publishes to XDG only, so the acceptance criterion as written is only partially satisfied. No direct child items or dependency blockers are present.

| 2 | scheduler/engine load from AMPA_WORKFLOW_DESCRIPTOR > .worklog/ampa/workflow.json > ~/.config/opencode/.worklog/ampa/workflow.json > package docs | met | src/ampa/engine_factory.py:95 — resolves descriptor in the specified precedence order; src/ampa/scheduler.py:485 applies the same order when audit path needs to load a descriptor explicitly. |

audit.author: rgardler
audit.text:
Ready to close: No

## Summary
The installer now supports an explicit --docs-path and implements multiple fallback discovery strategies (git-based repo root, fixed relative path, and an upward search from SCRIPT_DIR then CWD). It also implements XDG publish logic and a --force-workflow flag to permit overwrites. However, if the canonical descriptor cannot be located by these searches the installer logs and skips publishing (there is no fallback that reads a bundled workflow.json from the installed package), so global publish is not guaranteed in all invocation contexts. A manual verifier script exists (skill/install-ampa/scripts/install-workflow-verify.sh) but there is no automated unit test exercising the global-publish path.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | A worklog item tracks the work. | met | wl show AM-0MNP2DAWM00006JD --children --json — work item present |
| 2 | Implement robust fallback search for docs/workflow/workflow.json if REPO_ROOT detection fails (search upwards from CWD, or accept explicit docs path argument). | met | skill/install-ampa/scripts/install-worklog-plugin.sh:1366-1376 — upward-search loop from SCRIPT_DIR then CWD (search_limit=8); parse_args supports --docs-path. |
| 3 | Installer reliably publishes the canonical JSON to XDG when run in global mode (without manual repo path hacks); skip overwrite unless --force provided. | partial | skill/install-ampa/scripts/install-worklog-plugin.sh:1476 & 184 — XDG publish copies repo_workflow_json when found and skips overwrite unless --force-workflow; if repo_workflow_json unset it logs 'skipping XDG publish' and does not check installed package bundle. |

## Children Status

No children.

data.description:
Problem: Global installer runs do not reliably publish the canonical workflow descriptor (docs/workflow/workflow.json) into the XDG config location (/home/rgardler/.config/opencode/.worklog/ampa/workflow.json). The installer reported 'Canonical workflow descriptor not found' in a previous run even though the file exists in the repo.

Goal: Make the installer reliably publish the canonical docs/workflow/workflow.json during global installs (and when TARGET_DIR is explicitly set), add robust REPO_ROOT detection/fallbacks, and optionally add a --force flag and schema validation.

Proposed tasks:
1) Investigate current REPO_ROOT detection in skill/install-ampa/scripts/install-worklog-plugin.sh and reproduce failure mode for global installs.
2) Implement robust fallback search for docs/workflow/workflow.json if REPO_ROOT detection fails (search upwards from CWD, or accept explicit docs path argument). Prefer minimal change.
3) Ensure the installer copies docs/workflow/workflow.json to /home/rgardler/.config/opencode/.worklog/ampa/workflow.json for global installs and to .worklog/ampa/workflow.json for local installs, skipping overwrite unless --force is provided.
4) Add optional JSON schema validation (using existing workflow-schema.json) before copying — make this optional.
5) Add tests/harness that verify publishing behavior for both local and global installs.

Acceptance criteria:
- A worklog item tracks the work.
- Installer reliably publishes the canonical JSON to XDG when run in global mode (without manual repo path hacks) in our environment.
- Test or manual verification steps added and documented.

References:
- skill/install-ampa/scripts/install-worklog-plugin.sh
- docs/workflow/workflow.json
- skill/install-ampa/resources/ampa_py/ampa/docs/workflow/workflow-schema.json
- Previously-observed failing tests: tests/test_scheduler_audit_routing.py
data.title: Installer: ensure global install publishes canonical docs/workflow/workflow.json to XDG

data.description:
Scheduled wl-audit notifications sent to Discord are currently not actionable. For failed audits the message can be terse (for example: ) and does not include failed acceptance criteria details. Current audit notifications are also mostly file attachments without a concise inline summary.

User story:
As an operator monitoring scheduled audits in Discord,
I want clear inline audit summaries and explicit failed acceptance criteria details,
So that I can quickly understand what failed and whether an item is ready to merge without opening attachments.

Expected behavior:
- For audit failures, Discord message body includes full details of unmet acceptance criteria (criterion text, verdict, evidence).
- Audit notifications include a concise summary section inline (title, work item id, closure/ready-to-merge status, criteria pass/fail counts).
- Full report attachments may still be included, but they must augment (not replace) the inline summary.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] When a scheduled wl-audit run records a non-closure recommendation, the Discord message includes explicit details for each unmet acceptance criterion.
- [ ] Audit Discord messages include an inline summary with work item title, work item id, and ready-to-merge/closure recommendation status.
- [ ] Existing scheduler/audit tests are updated or added to validate the new message content for both passing and failing audit outcomes.
- [ ] Relevant test suite passes.
data.title: Improve wl-audit Discord notifications for failed criteria and summary
