## Summary
The investigation finds no installer code that directly deletes <project>/.worklog/ampa/.env. The installer reads/writes plugin .env files (TARGET_DIR/ampa_py/ampa/.env) and uses a backup/restore flow; an earlier backup-location bug (backups placed inside the plugin tree) was the likely indirect cause of lost backups. The recent commit and added test back up plugin and project envs to /tmp/ampa_env_backups and restore them, satisfying the acceptance criteria.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | Identify code paths that read/write/delete .worklog/ampa/.env and plugin .env files. | met | skill/install-ampa/scripts/install-worklog-plugin.sh:632-651 — defines backup_env_file (default backup_dir=/tmp/ampa_env_backups) and logs BACKUP_ENV. |
| 2 | Confirm whether the current installer can directly delete .worklog/ampa/.env. | met | skill/install-ampa/scripts/install-worklog-plugin.sh:866 — rm -rf "$py_target_dir/ampa" (removes plugin tree, not .worklog/ampa). No rm targeting .worklog/ampa present; migration preserves project .worklog/ampa (lines 478-486). |
| 3 | If direct deletion is not present, identify likely indirect causes and evidence. | met | .git/logs/refs/heads/main:36 — commit message "Preserve project .env during installer runs" and skill/install-ampa/scripts/install-worklog-plugin.sh:848-851,855-861,921-924 — backup/restore of plugin .env and scheduler_store to /tmp and restore. Test evidence: skill/install-ampa/tests/test_installer_publish_workflow.py:67-98 (test_env_preserved_across_upgrade). |
| 4 | Provide actionable remediation steps. | met | skill/install-ampa/scripts/install-worklog-plugin.sh:635-646 — backups go to /tmp/ampa_env_backups and log_decision records BACKUP_ENV; git commit dffbc1188df9c0d2944da2aa62409ccd4469fdda implements the fix and added test (see .git/logs/refs/heads/main:36). |

## Children Status

No children.

## Summary
The new audit output includes the work item Title/ID and a single-line Ready to close token; unit tests cover formatting and ready-to-close logic. The scheduler and handlers attach the full audit markdown as an in-memory attachment/metadata, but the scheduler does not persist the audit markdown to a filesystem path nor print that path, and repository documentation was not updated.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | Audit command output includes the work item title and ID for every candidate it reports. | met | src/ampa/audit/handlers.py:267-269 — `_format_audit_discord_summary` emits `- Title: ...` and `- ID: ...`; src/ampa/scheduler.py:694-714 — `_send_audit_notification` builds title_with_id and attachment filename `audit-<id>.md`. |
| 2 | Audit command output includes a single-line statement: "Ready to close: YES" or "Ready to close: NO". When NO, a one-paragraph summary of missing actions must follow. | met | src/ampa/audit/handlers.py:263-271,289-306 — ready-to-close token (`Ready to close: {YES|NO}`) and synthesized missing-actions paragraph are composed in `_format_audit_discord_summary`. |
| 3 | The full audit markdown is saved as a separate attached file (path included in output) and the command prints the path. | partial | src/ampa/scheduler.py:710-714 — scheduler creates an in-memory attachment dict with filename `audit-<id>.md` and content but does not write to `.worklog/audit` nor print a filesystem path; src/ampa/audit/handlers.py:551-560,596 — handler returns `metadata["audit_report_markdown"]` so callers can attach it. No code writes the markdown to a persistent .worklog/audit file. |
| 4 | Unit or integration tests cover the new output formatting and the ready-to-close determination logic. | met | tests/test_audit_handlers.py:400-416,432-444 — tests assert summary contains Title, ID, Ready to close token and audit_report_markdown metadata; end-to-end handler tests exercise success and failure paths. |

data.description:
Headline
Enhance wl-audit output to include work item title, ID, and a clear ready-to-close statement while still attaching the full audit markdown.

Problem statement
The audit command currently emits only raw audit markdown. Operators should receive a concise, machine-friendly summary (work item title and ID and a ready-to-close statement) plus the full audit attached as a file to enable faster triage and decision-making.

Users
- Operators and maintainers who run the wl-audit scheduled command and consume its output (support engineers).
- Developers and maintainers who rely on clear audit outputs to triage and fix issues.

User stories
- As an operator, I want the audit output to show the work item title and ID so I can quickly identify the affected work item.
- As an operator, I want a clear "ready to close" statement so I can decide whether to close or escalate the item.
- As a developer, I want the full audit markdown attached so I can inspect raw evidence while also seeing a concise summary.

Success criteria
- Audit command output includes the work item title and ID for every candidate it reports.
- Audit command output includes a single-line statement: "Ready to close: YES" or "Ready to close: NO". When NO, a one-paragraph summary of missing actions must follow.
- The full audit markdown is saved as a separate attached file (path included in output) and the command prints the path.
- Unit or integration tests cover the new output formatting and the ready-to-close determination logic.
- All related documentation (README, code comments, and any relevant wiki pages) is updated.

Example output (machine-friendly and human summary):
```
Candidate: AM-0MNG2ZIIE001EOUQ — Fix: audit_poller persist last_audit only on handler success
Ready to close: NO
Missing actions: Add unit test to assert last_audit persists only on handler success; update handler to record last_attempt when failing.
Full audit saved: .worklog/audit/AM-0MNG2ZIIE001EOUQ-2026-04-03.md
```

Constraints
- Must not change the existing audit semantics or the underlying determination logic for readiness-to-close, only present additional information.
- Changes must be backward-compatible for downstream tools that may parse the primary audit output (preserve machine-parsable fields or provide a compatibility flag).
- Avoid large refactors; prefer small changes to the output formatter and add tests.

Minimal tests to add:
- Formatter unit test asserting title, id, ready-to-close line, and attachment path are present.
- Integration test that runs wl-audit against a mocked candidate and confirms the output and file attachment behavior.

Risks & assumptions
- Risk: Investigation could expose sensitive log content. Mitigation: request a sanitized log or access via a secure channel.
- Risk: Multiple related issues (descriptor validation, state handling) could be conflated, increasing scope. Mitigation: separate diagnosis steps and create distinct follow-up work items for fixes.
- Risk: Changing output format could break downstream consumers that parse current output. Mitigation: keep a machine-parsable summary line or add a compatibility flag and include parsing tests.
- Risk: Large attachments could cause disk usage or retention issues. Mitigation: limit stored audit file size and document retention.
- Assumption: The ready-to-close heuristic exists; if not, creating it is out of scope and should be a separate follow-up task.


Existing state
 - The wl-audit scheduled command currently prints only the raw audit markdown to stdout and stores the daemon log at .worklog/ampa/default/default.log.
 - Related work items exist for audit_poller fixes and scheduler commands; this task should be a child of AM-0MNK8VAT4004SZBS.
 - Recent daemon log entries show workflow descriptor validation failures referencing
   `~/.config/opencode/.worklog/plugins/ampa_py/ampa/docs/workflow/workflow.yaml` and a missing schema `/docs/workflow/workflow-schema.json`.

Desired change
- Update the audit command's output formatter to include: work item title, work item ID, and a single-line ready-to-close statement. When not ready, include a concise missing-actions summary.
- Continue to save the full audit markdown to a file and print its path in the command output.
- Add tests that assert the presence and format of the new fields and the attachment behavior.

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNK8VAT4004SZBS — Ensure audit is working as expected. Parent of this item; provides broader acceptance criteria and context for the audit output changes.
- AM-0MNFSNJ3W00787T5 — Task: wl-audit scheduler command. Implements and coordinates the wl-audit command which this change affects; useful for understanding CLI and attachment behaviour.
- AM-0MNG2ZIIE001EOUQ — Fix: audit_poller persist last_audit only on handler success. Changes to poller persist semantics may affect audit outputs; review for compatibility.
- AM-0MNN03C9F00456R4 — Investigate audit poller selection mismatch. Investigation that may surface edge cases which the audit output should represent.
- AM-0MNG2ZILA006V7AX — Fix: audit_poller JSON parsing robustness & invalid JSON handling. Relevant where audit attachments include JSON or parsing influences output formatting.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
- Q: "Do any existing work items represent the work to gather evidence from the daemon default.log?" 1 Answer (user): "Use existing: Ensure audit is working as expected (AM-0MNK8VAT4004SZBS) (Recommended)". Source: interactive selection. Final: yes.

Effort and Risk (initial estimate)
- Summary: Small, localized formatter change estimated at ~15 effort points (base 10 + 5 overhead). Certainty: 70%.
- Ready-to-close: No (insufficient acceptance criteria and unknowns remain: ready-to-close heuristic details and downstream consumers).
- Key reasoning: Base estimate from O/M/P (1/3/6) with coordination, review, testing and a small risk buffer; unknowns increase required validation work.


# Discord delivery (explicit)
- The formatted summary must be delivered via the AMPA Discord bot to the channel configured in the AMPA configuration. The Discord message must contain the candidate line (ID + title), the single-line "Ready to close: YES/NO", and when NO a one-paragraph missing-actions summary. The full audit markdown must be uploaded as a file attachment with filename pattern `audit-<work-item-id>-<timestamp>.md`. For long audits, do not paste full markdown into the message body — attach the file and include the short summary in the Discord message.

# Acceptance criterion (Discord)
- Discord posting: The AMPA bot posts the formatted summary to the configured Discord channel and uploads the full audit markdown as a file attachment. Integration test must verify the bot posts the correct summary and attaches the file (Discord API mocked). The message body should not contain the full audit markdown — attach the file and include only the short summary in the message.

# Clarifying Q&A (recent answers)
- Q: "Delivery target — should the bot post the summary to Discord?" — Answer (user): Yes; post to the channel configured in the config. Source: interactive reply (user). Final: post to configured channel.
- Q: "Full-audit handling — how to deliver the full markdown?" — Answer (user): Attach as file upload in Discord. Source: interactive reply (user). Final: attach file upload in Discord.
- Q: "Mentions/pings — should the message ping anyone?" — Answer (user): No (do not ping). Source: interactive reply (user). Final: do not ping.
data.title: Add audit command output: include title, ID, and closure status; attach full audit markdown

audit.author: rgardler
audit.text:
Ready to close: Yes

## Summary
The descriptor schema and parser include an explicit `agent` field and round-trip unit tests verify the field is preserved. The parser and CommandSpec implementation (to_dict/from_dict) already round-trip the `agent` field and a unit test exercises this behavior. With missing-agent validation removed from the acceptance criteria, the remaining requirements are satisfied and the work item is ready to close.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | Descriptor schema accepts an 'agent' or 'model_agent' property and parser extracts it. | met | src/ampa/scheduler_types.py:100-129 — CommandSpec.agent defined; to_dict()/from_dict() include and preserve the field. |
| 2 | Unit tests assert parsing extracts agent values (round-trip) and parsing behaviour is exercised in CI. | met | tests/test_scheduler_types_from_iso.py:29-46 — test_command_spec_round_trip_includes_agent_field asserts payload['agent'] == 'Casey' and restored.agent == 'Casey'. |

## Children Status

No children.

data.description:
Summary:
Add an explicit 'agent' field to scheduled command descriptors so the scheduler can require and surface a model/agent name for scheduled runs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Descriptor schema accepts an 'agent' or 'model_agent' property and parser extracts it.
- Unit tests assert parsing extracts agent values (round-trip) and parsing behaviour is exercised in CI.

## Minimal Implementation
- Extend descriptor schema and parser in src/ampa/scheduler_types.py to include 'agent' (already implemented).
- Surface parsed agent value to executor-visible execution spec (already present).
- Add or ensure unit tests that create descriptors and assert parser behaviour (round-trip test exists).

## Prototype / Experiment
- Small unit test suite that validates parsing behaviour. Success = tests pass locally and in CI.

## Dependencies
- None (foundational).

## Deliverables
- Code changes in src/ampa/scheduler_types.py and any descriptor helpers.
- Unit tests under tests/ for descriptor parsing and validation (parsing-only).
- Docs snippet (docs/scheduler-local-llm.md) showing descriptor example.
data.title: Command Descriptor Agent Field

audit.author: rgardler
audit.text:
Ready to close: Yes

## Summary

The executor agent-resolution changes are implemented and covered by unit tests: the resolver maps agent identifiers to endpoints, the default probe accepts an optional agent parameter, and tests assert the probe is called with the resolved agent and endpoint. Backwards compatibility for probes that accept only a single URL argument is handled and exercised by existing tests. Based on the code and tests reviewed, the work item can be closed.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | Executor resolves an 'agent' identifier to an endpoint URL via a local mapping. | met | src/ampa/scheduler_executor.py:139-150 — resolve_llm_probe_target returns (agent, endpoint) using agent_endpoints mapping. |
| 2 | default_llm_probe accepts an optional agent parameter and returns the resolved endpoint (mockable). | met | src/ampa/scheduler_executor.py:122-136 — default_llm_probe(url, agent=None) exists and delegates to _default_llm_probe_impl that logs agent and performs the request. |
| 3 | Unit tests assert executor calls llm_probe with the provided agent and uses the returned endpoint. | met | tests/test_scheduler_scoring.py:219-231 — test_scheduler_resolves_configured_agent_endpoint_for_probe sets scheduler.llm_probe and asserts calls == [("https://llm.example.com/health", "Riley")]. |
| 4 | Behaviour for commands without 'agent' remains unchanged unless debug/local-only validation triggers an error per descriptor rules. | met | src/ampa/scheduler.py:312-322 — _probe_llm falls back to one-arg probe on TypeError; tests use single-arg llm_probe lambdas (tests/test_scheduler_scoring.py:40,98) exercising backward compatibility. |

## Children Status

No children.

data.description:
Summary:
Resolve the 'agent' from the command spec to a configured endpoint and pass it to default_llm_probe so scheduled runs use the mapped endpoint.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Executor resolves an 'agent' identifier to an endpoint URL via a local mapping.
- default_llm_probe accepts an optional agent parameter and returns the resolved endpoint (mockable).
- Unit tests assert executor calls llm_probe with the provided agent and uses the returned endpoint.
- Behaviour for commands without 'agent' remains unchanged unless debug/local-only validation triggers an error per descriptor rules.

## Minimal Implementation
- Implement a small resolver in src/ampa/scheduler_executor.py that reads mapping from scheduler config or a simple in-memory dict for tests.
- Update calls to default_llm_probe to pass the agent identifier.
- Add unit tests mocking default_llm_probe.

## Prototype / Experiment
- Unit test with a fake mapping dict and mocked default_llm_probe. Success = probe called with provided agent and returned endpoint used.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Command Descriptor Agent Field (AM-0MNS4V1DB004SNU9).

## Deliverables
- Code, unit tests, example mapping in README or docs.
data.title: Executor Agent Resolution

audit.author: rgardler
audit.text:
Ready to close: No

## Summary

Descriptor validation tests, CI integration, and documentation updates required by this work item are not present. The scheduler resolves unspecified descriptor agents to the default agent (Casey) at runtime instead of treating missing-agent descriptors as validation errors in debug/local-only mode. As a result, the acceptance criteria are not satisfied and the item cannot be closed.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | Tests assert parser/validator accepts well-formed descriptors and rejects missing-agent descriptors when required by debug/local-only mode. | unmet | src/ampa/scheduler_executor.py:147 — resolve_llm_probe_target falls back to configured/default agent or Casey, so missing-agent descriptors are accepted by default. |
| 2 | New tests run as part of the existing test suite without external LLM dependencies. | unmet | tests/descriptor_test.py: missing — no descriptor-specific test file present; existing tests mock llm_probe patterns (tests/test_scheduler_scoring.py:40) but do not assert missing-agent rejection. |
| 3 | CI notes updated to include these tests. | unmet | .github/workflows/ci.yml:93 — CI runs pytest; src/ampa/README.md:62 — docs/config mention default agent but do not document descriptor validation tests or CI inclusion. |

## Children Status

No children.

audit.author: rgardler
audit.text:
Ready to close: Yes

## Summary
The scheduler logs a warning when an agent endpoint resolves to a non-local URL and a unit test asserts this behaviour via caplog. Both acceptance criteria are implemented in code and covered by an existing unit test; no unresolved dependencies or children were found.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | When an agent mapping resolves to a non-local endpoint, executor logs a WARNING including command id, agent name, and resolved endpoint. | met | src/ampa/scheduler.py:303-310 — LOG.warning emits "Resolved non-local LLM endpoint" with command_id, agent, endpoint |
| 2 | Unit tests use caplog to assert warning emission on non-local resolution. | met | tests/test_scheduler_scoring.py:234-260 — `test_scheduler_warns_when_agent_endpoint_is_non_local` sets a remote endpoint and asserts caplog contains the warning |

## Children Status

No children.

data.description:
# Automated Intake Scheduled Task

**Headline**: Automate the intake process for work items in the "idea" stage by creating a scheduled task that queries Worklog, spawns opencode `/intake` sessions, and auto-assigns completed items to appropriate agents.

## Problem Statement

Currently, work items in the "idea" stage require manual intervention to progress through the intake process. An operator must manually identify items needing intake, run the `/intake` command, and assign them to appropriate agents. This creates a bottleneck and delays work item progression.

## Users

- **Project Maintainers/Operators**: Who need to ensure work items progress smoothly through the intake pipeline
- **Developers/Contributors**: Who create work items and want them processed efficiently
- **AMPA System**: Automated agent that manages work item lifecycle

## User Stories

- As a project maintainer, I want the intake process to run automatically so that work items in the "idea" stage are processed without manual intervention
- As a developer, I want my newly created work items to be automatically processed and assigned to appropriate agents so they can start work quickly
- As an operator, I want the system to handle the intake interview and documentation generation so I don't have to manually run `/intake` commands for each item
- As a maintainer, I want items with insufficient information to be flagged with "input_needed" status and clear questions so the requester knows what to provide

## Success Criteria

1. Scheduled task runs at a configurable interval (e.g., every 15 minutes) to find work items in the "idea" stage
2. Task selects the highest sort-order item from the "idea" stage for processing
3. Task spawns an opencode session with `/intake <id>` command and "Do not ask any questions" instruction
4. If questions arise during intake, they are recorded as open questions in the work item and **status** is set to "input_needed"
5. If intake completes successfully, the work item stage is set to "intake_complete"
6. When stage is "intake_complete", the system automatically assigns the work item to the most appropriate agent based on work type, priority, and agent capabilities
7. Assignment logic considers: work item type (bug/feature/task), priority level, required skills, and agent availability
8. Task handles errors gracefully (e.g., opencode session failures, network issues) without breaking the scheduler
9. Logging and monitoring are in place to track intake processing success/failure rates
10. The feature is idempotent - running multiple times doesn't create duplicate assignments or cause conflicts

## Constraints

- Must integrate with existing AMPA scheduler architecture
- Must use the existing dispatch system (`OpenCodeRunDispatcher`) for spawning opencode sessions
- Must respect Worklog workflow conventions for stage transitions and status values
- Must handle cases where no items are in "idea" stage gracefully
- Must not interfere with manually-triggered intake sessions
- Should avoid creating duplicate assignments if an item is already assigned
- Must handle the case where agent assignment cannot be determined (fallback to manual assignment)
- **Note**: "input_needed" must be implemented as a **status** (not a stage) and work items can be in any stage while in this status

## Existing State

- AMPA scheduler system exists with `Scheduler`, `SchedulerStore`, and executor infrastructure
- Dispatch system (`OpenCodeRunDispatcher`) can spawn opencode sessions with `/intake` command
- Worklog supports stage transitions (idea → intake_complete → plan_complete → in_progress → in_review → done)
- Worklog supports statuses: open, in-progress, completed, blocked, deleted
- Work items have sort ordering capabilities via `sortIndex` field
- Agent assignment system exists but may need enhancement for automatic assignment
- Existing scheduled tasks: wl-audit, cleanup-runner, QA nightly runner (patterns to follow)
- Parent epic AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD for re-adding scheduled tasks incrementally

## Desired Change

Implement a new scheduled task type "intake-runner" that:

1. **Query Phase**: Queries Worklog for work items in "idea" stage, sorted by `sortIndex` descending
2. **Selection Phase**: Selects the highest priority item (highest sort-order)
3. **Dispatch Phase**: Spawns an opencode session via `OpenCodeRunDispatcher` with command:
   ```
   opencode run "/intake <work-item-id> Do not ask any questions. If you have questions enter them as open questions in the issue and set the item to the status 'input_needed'"
   ```
4. **Assignment Phase**: Monitors work item for stage change to "intake_complete"
5. **Auto-Assignment**: When intake_complete is reached, determines appropriate agent based on:
   - Work item type (bug/feature/task/epic)
   - Priority level (critical/high/medium/low)
   - Tags/skill requirements
   - Agent availability and workload
6. **Completion**: Updates work item assignee and stage to ready for implementation

## Risks & Assumptions

**Risks:**
- **Scope creep**: Agent assignment logic could become complex with many heuristics. *Mitigation: Start with simple rule-based assignment (type + priority), document advanced ML-based assignment as future work*
- **Infinite loops**: If intake fails repeatedly, could create spam. *Mitigation: Add retry limits and backoff strategy*
- **Agent overload**: Automatic assignment could overwhelm agents. *Mitigation: Check agent workload before assignment, implement queue limits*
- **Race conditions**: Multiple scheduled task runs could conflict. *Mitigation: Implement locking mechanism or idempotency checks*
- **Dependency on opencode availability**: If opencode is not installed or fails, intake cannot proceed. *Mitigation: Add health checks and graceful degradation*

**Assumptions:**
- The `/intake` command in opencode exists and functions as expected
- Agent information (skills, availability) is accessible via Worklog or AMPA configuration
- The scheduler can reliably detect stage changes after dispatch
- Work items have meaningful sortIndex values for prioritization
- The system has appropriate permissions to update work item stage and assignee

## Related Work

- **AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD**: "Strip all scheduled tasks, re-add one at a time" (parent epic) - The scheduler re-architecture work this task must integrate with
- **AM-0MNFSNJ3W00787T5**: "Task: wl-audit scheduler command" (high priority) - Similar scheduled task pattern to follow
- **AM-0MNFSNA5B004ULRI**: "cleanup-runner scheduler command" (high priority) - Similar scheduled task pattern
- **AM-0MNFT4B0A003278C**: "QA nightly runner - scheduled task" (blocked) - Pattern for scheduled tasks
- **AM-0MNO8G0NH00956CY**: "Scheduler: add 'agent' field and default Casey agent" (completed) - Agent field implementation reference
- `src/ampa/scheduler.py`: Core scheduler implementation
- `src/ampa/scheduler_executor.py`: Task execution logic
- `src/ampa/engine/dispatch.py`: OpenCodeRunDispatcher for spawning sessions
- `src/ampa/scheduler_helpers.py`: Helper functions for scheduled commands

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers

- **Q**: "How often should the intake scheduled task run?"
  - **Answer (agent inference)**: Based on existing patterns (cleanup-runner, wl-audit), every 15 minutes is appropriate for near-real-time processing without overwhelming the system
  
- **Q**: "What criteria should be used for agent assignment?"
  - **Answer (agent inference)**: Start with work item type and priority. Map bugs to Casey (QA agent), features to appropriate dev agents based on tags. Document advanced heuristic assignment as future work
  
- **Q**: "What happens if no agents are available for assignment?"
  - **Answer (agent inference)**: Leave assignee blank but mark stage as intake_complete. Create a work item or notification for operators to manually assign
  
- **Q**: "Should the task process multiple items per run or one at a time?"
  - **Answer (agent inference)**: One at a time to avoid overwhelming opencode sessions and to allow proper error handling per item. The scheduler will pick up the next item on the next run
  
- **Q**: "Should 'input_needed' be a stage or a status?"
  - **Answer (user)**: "input_needed" should be a **status**, not a stage. A work item can be in any stage when it has the "input_needed" status.
  - **Action**: Updated AM-0MNU7MXP40060TW7 description to use status instead of stage. Created child work item to add "input_needed" status to Worklog system.

## Effort and Risk (initial estimate)

- **Effort**: Medium - estimated ~12-16 hours. Requires scheduler integration, dispatch system usage, agent assignment logic, and testing
- **Risk**: Medium - Integration points with scheduler and opencode create potential failure modes. Need robust error handling and monitoring
- **Certainty**: 75% - Core patterns exist, but agent assignment logic requires some discovery
data.title: Automated intake scheduled task

data.description:
# Add "input_needed" Status to Worklog System

## Problem Statement

The Worklog system currently supports a limited set of statuses (open, in-progress, completed, blocked, deleted). The automated intake process requires a new "input_needed" status to indicate when a work item requires additional information from the requester before it can proceed. Currently, this state cannot be properly represented in the system.

## Users

- **Project Maintainers**: Who need to track which work items are awaiting input
- **Developers/Requesters**: Who create work items and need to know when more information is required
- **AMPA Automated System**: Which needs to set this status programmatically during intake processing

## User Stories

- As a project maintainer, I want to see which work items are in "input_needed" status so I can follow up with requesters for missing information
- As a developer, I want to know when my work item needs more information so I can provide the necessary details
- As an automated system, I want to set the "input_needed" status when the intake process identifies missing information

## Success Criteria

1. The "input_needed" status is added to the Worklog system's valid status values
2. Work items can be filtered and listed by "input_needed" status using `wl list --status input_needed`
3. The status can be set and updated via `wl update <id> --status input_needed`
4. The new status is compatible with all existing stages (idea, intake_complete, plan_complete, in_progress, in_review, done)
5. The status appears correctly in the TUI and other Worklog interfaces
6. Full test suite passes with the new status
7. All related documentation is updated to reflect the new status

## Constraints

- Must be backward compatible with existing status values
- Must not break existing work items or workflows
- Must integrate cleanly with the existing Worklog data model
- Should follow the same patterns as existing statuses (open, in-progress, completed, blocked, deleted)

## Existing State

- Worklog currently supports statuses: open, in-progress, completed, blocked, deleted
- Worklog supports stages: idea, intake_complete, plan_complete, in_progress, in_review, done
- The status and stage fields operate independently - a work item has both a status and a stage
- Status is tracked in the work item database schema
- Status transitions are validated by the Worklog system

## Desired Change

Add "input_needed" as a new valid status value in the Worklog system:

1. Update the status validation/schema to include "input_needed"
2. Ensure the status is accepted by `wl create` and `wl update` commands
3. Update any status enumeration or type definitions in the codebase
4. Add appropriate tests for the new status
5. Update documentation to list the new status option

## Risks & Assumptions

**Risks:**
- **Backward compatibility**: Existing tools or integrations might not recognize the new status. *Mitigation: Ensure the status is purely additive and doesn't change existing behavior*
- **UI/UX confusion**: Users might confuse "input_needed" status with stages. *Mitigation: Clear documentation and help text explaining the distinction*

**Assumptions:**
- The Worklog status system is extensible and supports adding new statuses
- The status field is stored as a string or enum that can be extended
- No migration of existing work items is required

## Related Work

- **AM-0MNU7MXP40060TW7**: "Automated intake scheduled task" (parent) - The work item that requires this new status
- **AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD**: "Strip all scheduled tasks, re-add one at a time" - Parent epic
- Worklog CLI source code for status handling
- Worklog database schema

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers

- **Q**: "Should 'input_needed' be a stage or a status?"
  - **Answer (user)**: "input_needed" should be a **status**, not a stage. A work item can be in any stage when it has the "input_needed" status.
  
- **Q**: "What existing statuses are supported?"
  - **Answer (agent inference from `wl create --help`)**: open, in-progress, completed, blocked, deleted
data.title: Add 'input_needed' status to Worklog system

data.description:
# Intake Query and Selection

**Headline**: Implement a candidate selector that queries Worklog for "idea" stage work items and returns the highest priority item for intake processing.

## Problem Statement

The automated intake system needs a mechanism to identify and select work items in the "idea" stage that are ready for intake processing. Currently, there is no standardized way to query Worklog for candidate items and select the highest priority one for processing.

## Users

- **AMPA Scheduler**: Automated system that needs to find and process work items awaiting intake
- **Project Maintainers**: Who need visibility into which work items are queued for intake
- **Developers**: Who create work items and want them processed in priority order

## User Stories

- As the AMPA scheduler, I want to query Worklog for work items in "idea" stage so I can identify candidates for intake processing
- As the AMPA scheduler, I want to select the highest priority item based on sortIndex so that more important work items are processed first
- As a project maintainer, I want the intake process to handle empty result sets gracefully so the scheduler continues operating normally when no items need intake
- As a developer, I want my work items to be processed in priority order so urgent items get attention first

## Success Criteria

1. Query returns items with stage="idea" sorted by sortIndex descending (highest priority first)
2. Selects single highest priority item per run (one at a time to avoid overwhelming opencode sessions)
3. Handles empty result set gracefully (no-op, no errors)
4. Respects scheduler global rate limiting
5. Unit tests cover selection logic and edge cases
6. All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries
7. Full project test suite must pass with the new changes

## Constraints

- Must integrate with existing AMPA scheduler architecture
- Must use existing Worklog CLI commands (`wl list --stage idea --json`)
- Must follow patterns established by existing auto-registered commands (wl-audit, pr-monitor)
- Must not process multiple items per run to avoid overwhelming opencode sessions
- Must be idempotent - running multiple times shouldn't cause duplicate processing

## Existing State

- AMPA scheduler system exists with `Scheduler`, `SchedulerStore`, and executor infrastructure
- Worklog supports stage filtering via `wl list --stage <stage>`
- Work items have `sortIndex` field for prioritization
- Existing auto-registered commands (wl-audit, pr-monitor) provide patterns to follow
- Scheduler helpers module (`scheduler_helpers.py`) contains command registration utilities
- The scheduler uses `CommandSpec` to define scheduled commands

## Desired Change

Implement an `IntakeCandidateSelector` class that:

1. **Query Phase**: Executes `wl list --stage idea --json` to fetch all work items in "idea" stage
2. **Sorting Phase**: Sorts results by `sortIndex` descending (highest priority first)
3. **Selection Phase**: Returns the top item (highest sortIndex) or None if no items exist
4. **Integration**: Works with the scheduler to be called at configurable intervals

The component will be part of the intake-runner scheduled task ecosystem and provide the query/selection foundation for the full intake workflow.

## Risks & Assumptions

**Risks:**
- **Worklog CLI changes**: If `wl list` output format changes, parsing could break. *Mitigation: Use structured JSON parsing with validation*
- **Performance at scale**: Large numbers of "idea" stage items could slow queries. *Mitigation: Consider pagination or limiting if needed; initial implementation can be simple*
- **Race conditions**: Item could change stage between query and selection. *Mitigation: This is acceptable - the next run will pick up the next item*
- **Scope creep**: Could expand to include complex filtering or batch processing. *Mitigation: Record opportunities for additional features (complex filtering, batch processing, ML-based prioritization) as separate work items linked to this one, rather than expanding the scope of the current item*

**Assumptions:**
- Worklog CLI `wl list --stage idea --json` returns valid JSON with work item array
- `sortIndex` field exists and is numeric on all work items
- The scheduler handles rate limiting globally; this component focuses only on query/selection
- Work items in "idea" stage are ready for intake processing (no additional pre-checks needed)

## Related Work

- **AM-0MNU7MXP40060TW7**: "Automated intake scheduled task" (parent) - Overall intake automation epic
- **AM-0MNUB4V80006OGMK**: "Intake dispatch mechanism" (sibling) - Dispatches opencode sessions after selection
- **AM-0MNU8KC46006G5F4**: "Add 'input_needed' status to Worklog system" (dependency) - Required status for intake workflow
- **AM-0MNUB5351000HNC9**: "Intake completion detection" (sibling) - Monitors intake results
- **AM-0MNUB5AK4000V1V2**: "Tag-based agent assignment" (sibling) - Assigns agents after intake
- **AM-0MNUB5GOC002L4OX**: "Intake runner command" (sibling) - Orchestrates full workflow
- `src/ampa/scheduler_helpers.py`: Pattern for auto-registered commands
- `src/ampa/scheduler_store.py`: Scheduler state management
- `tests/test_scheduler_audit_routing.py`: Testing patterns for scheduler components

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers

- **Q**: "Should the intake task process multiple items per run, or one at a time?"
  - **Answer (user)**: One item at a time (to avoid overwhelming opencode sessions)
  - **Source**: Interactive reply during planning
  - **Final**: Yes

- **Q**: "For agent auto-assignment, which approach for the first iteration?"
  - **Answer (user)**: Option B: Tag-based lookup in workflow descriptor
  - **Source**: Interactive reply during planning
  - **Final**: Yes

- **Q**: "Should the intake-runner command be auto-registered like wl-audit and pr-monitor?"
  - **Answer (user)**: Auto-registered (like wl-audit and pr-monitor)
  - **Source**: Interactive reply during planning
  - **Final**: Yes

- **Q**: "What should happen when no items are in 'idea' stage?"
  - **Answer (agent inference)**: Return None/empty result gracefully, allowing the scheduler to continue without error
  - **Source**: Pattern from existing scheduler commands
  - **Final**: Yes

- **Q**: "How should items be sorted when multiple have the same sortIndex?"
  - **Answer (agent inference)**: Secondary sort by creation date (newest first) or ID for deterministic ordering
  - **Source**: Common pattern for tie-breaking
  - **Final**: TBD - implementer can decide based on existing Worklog patterns

## Acceptance Criteria

## Minimal Implementation

## Dependencies

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers

data.description:
# Intake Completion Detection

Detect intake completion and input_needed status for dispatched runs; record metrics in SchedulerStore and handle 4-hour timeouts.

## Problem statement

Automation should detect when dispatched intake runs complete or require additional input so the scheduler can record metrics and surface items for follow-up.

## Summary
Detect stage transitions for dispatched intake runs and record outcomes (intake_complete or input_needed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detect when work item reaches "intake_complete" stage
- Detect when work item gets "input_needed" status
- Track dispatch-to-completion time in SchedulerStore
- Handle timeout cases (intake didn't complete within 4 hours)

## Minimal Implementation
- Poll work item status via `wl show <id> --json`
- Check for stage="intake_complete" or status="input_needed"
- Store completion tracking in SchedulerStore
- Unit tests for detection logic

## Dependencies
- Feature: Intake dispatch mechanism
- AM-0MNU8KC46006G5F4: Add 'input_needed' status to Worklog system

## Deliverables
- Code, tests, documentation

## Related work (automated report)
- AM-0MNU8KC46006G5F4 - Add 'input_needed' status to Worklog system: Adds the required status value and documentation; this work is a dependency for correct detection of input_needed status.
- AM-0MNU8VWBZ009ESF3 - Create Worklog Feature Request Specification: Specification used to request input_needed status addition to Worklog.
- AM-0MNU8W52S009VYLW - Add input_needed status to AMPA adapter: Adapter changes to make AMPA accept the new status.
- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 - Intake query and selection: Query/selection logic for which work items should be dispatched for intake; complementary to this detection task.
- AM-0MNU7MXP40060TW7 - Automated intake scheduled task: Parent task coordinating the intake pipeline; this item implements the detection and metrics portion.

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers
- Q: "What timeout should be used to consider an intake run failed (intake didn't complete)?"
  - Answer (user): "4 hours (Recommended)". Source: user interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "Where should the dispatch-to-completion time be stored/recorded?"
  - Answer (user): "SchedulerStore (Recommended)". Source: user interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "Should this work item implement only detection and metrics, or also perform automatic assignment when intake_complete?"
  - Answer (user): "Detection + metrics only — do NOT auto-assign (Recommended)". Source: user interactive reply. Final: yes.

## Constraints
- Must not auto-assign agents; detection only.
- Must not modify work item stage except when marking intake_complete or input_needed.

## Testing
- Unit tests covering detection logic and timeout behavior.
- Integration test demonstrating SchedulerStore write.

## Risks & Assumptions
- Risk: Scope creep if assignment logic is added later. Mitigation: Record assignment as separate follow-up work items.
- Risk: Missing status support in Worklog. Mitigation: Depends on AM-0MNU8KC46006G5F4; this item should fail gracefully if status unsupported.
- Assumption: SchedulerStore is available and writable by this task.

## Handoff
- Implementation notes: keep polling interval configurable, use exponential backoff on transient errors, and ensure idempotent updates to SchedulerStore.

data.description:
Problem statement
Build a new deterministic State×Stage (State/Status) agent-assignment system for intake and delegation automation. This is a new system (not a replacement of a pre-existing runtime) that will replace the previously-proposed tag-based design and instead implement a deterministic lookup keyed by work-item state and workflow stage/status.

Users
- Intake automation engineers and scheduler authors who need a deterministic assignment mechanism to route work items reliably.
- On-call and rotating owners (Archie, Patch, Build, Map) who will receive assignments from the new State/Status mapping.

Success criteria
- The AM-0MNUB5AK4000V1V2 work item title and description are updated to describe building a new State/Status assignment system and include a deterministic mapping table and fallback. (Verification: work item title and description updated.)
- The mapping contains the example rules: open/in-progress + plan_complete -> Patch; open/in-progress + intake_complete -> Archie. (Verification: mapping table in description.)
- A documented default mapping (Default -> Build) is present for undefined combinations. (Verification: description contains Default -> Build.)
- The assignment system is defined as a deterministic lookup (stored in workflow descriptor or static mapping) with no capacity checks. (Verification: implementation PR references this work item and uses the mapping.)
- All related documentation is updated and unit tests for mapping lookup and fallback are added and pass.

Constraints
- No capacity/availability checks are performed by the assigner; availability concerns are out-of-scope for this work and should be addressed by schedulers if required.

Existing state
- The earlier work item text proposed a tag-based system; the user has directed the project to build a new State/Status-based assignment system instead. Related intake and dispatch items exist and should be referenced for integration.

Desired change
- Rename the work item to reflect the new system and update the description to document the new State/Status assignment system, mapping, implementation notes, and fallback rules.

Proposed deterministic mapping (State × Stage → Assignee)

- State in {open, in-progress} + Stage == plan_complete -> Patch
- State in {open, in-progress} + Stage == intake_complete -> Archie
- State in {open, in-progress} + Stage == idea -> Map
- State in {open, in-progress} + Stage == in_review -> Patch
- State in {open, in-progress} + Stage == done -> Build
- State == blocked -> Build
- State == completed -> Build
- Default (any combination not listed above) -> Build

Implementation notes
- Persist the mapping as a simple lookup in the workflow descriptor or a small JSON/YAML file that the intake runner reads. Use the first exact match for (state, stage); otherwise fall back to default_assignee.
- No availability/capacity checks in the assigner. If later desired, implement capacity checks in the scheduler as a separate work item.

Related work
- AM-0MNU7MXP40060TW7 — Automated intake scheduled task
- AM-0MNUB5GOC002L4OX — Intake runner command
- AM-0MNUB5351000HNC9 — Intake completion detection
- AM-0MNUB4V80006OGMK — Intake dispatch mechanism
- AM-0MNFT0NHT0051BP9 — Delegation/auto-delegate scheduler commands

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "Should the assignment rule be a deterministic mapping table (State × Status -> assignee) stored in the workflow descriptor and consulted during intake, or should it be implemented as a small rule engine with precedence/fallbacks?"
  - Answer (user): "deterministic". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Q: "Confirm the exact states and statuses to consider for automatic assignment. From your message: states = open, in_progress; statuses = plan_complete, intake_complete. Are there any other state/status combinations we should include now (e.g., blocked, review, done), or should we limit this change to the combinations you supplied?"
  - Answer (user): "Examine the agents available and attempt to build a complete mapping, for any undefined mappings assign to Build". Source: interactive reply. Final: mapping built and undefined combinations mapped to Build.

- Q: "For capacity/availability handling: should the assigner perform a basic check (e.g., skip assignment if the target agent currently has >N assigned items) or just set the assignee deterministically and leave availability checks to the scheduler?"
  - Answer (user): "no availability checks". Source: interactive reply. Final: No availability checks.

- Q: "Are we replacing an existing runtime system or building a new State/Status assignment system?"
  - Answer (user): "No we are not replacing a current system we are building a new system, as the previous description was building a new tag system, we now want to build a state/status system instead." Source: interactive reply. Final: Build a new State/Status system (not a replacement).
data.title: State/Status-based agent assignment

data.description:
Headline Summary: Implement an intake-runner scheduler command that non-interactively selects an "idea" stage work item, dispatches a `do not ask questions` intake run, records outcomes and metrics, and logs failures for operator follow-up.

Problem statement

Create a concise, implementable intake brief for the existing work item "Intake runner command" (AM-0MNUB5GOC002L4OX). The intake must confirm whether the item contains sufficient information to proceed or record any missing information as OPEN QUESTIONS. The request includes the directive "do not ask questions" so the intake must not contact the requester; missing information must be recorded in the Appendix.

Users

- Operators / schedulers that will run the intake-runner command
- AMPA maintainers who will implement or review the intake-runner
- Engineers who will convert the intake into code and tests

User stories

- As an operator, I can run the intake-runner command to process one work item in the "idea" stage non-interactively.
- As a maintainer, I can review the intake brief to know the required code changes, tests and documentation updates.
- As an engineer, I can implement the intake-runner from the brief without needing further clarifications from the requester.

Success criteria

1. The work item description contains a concise Problem statement and at least three measurable Acceptance Criteria (below).
2. The intake-runner implementation can select a single "idea" stage work item and dispatch an automated `/intake <id> do not ask questions` run. (Testable: unit/integration tests exercise selection and dispatch.)
3. The intake-runner handles errors and records outcomes (intake_complete, input_needed) and metrics. (Testable: simulated failure recorded in SchedulerStore.)
4. All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes (code comments, README, and any relevant docs). (Manual verification: changed files listed in PR.)
5. Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.
6. The selection logic includes unit tests to cover selection edge cases (empty set, ties, malformed items).
7. Dispatch logs include a unique dispatch id and ISO8601 timestamp for traceability. (Measurable: log entries contain dispatch_id and timestamp.)

Constraints

- Do not prompt the original requester; any missing information must be recorded as OPEN QUESTIONS in the Appendix and the item must be left in a state where operators can follow up.
- The runner should process one item per run to avoid overloading opencode sessions.
- Implementation must reuse existing scheduler and worklog APIs; avoid introducing new external services.

Existing state

- Work item AM-0MNUB5GOC002L4OX exists with title "Intake runner command" and is assigned to Map at stage=idea status=in-progress.
- There are completed and open items that describe intake selection, dispatch, detection, and supporting statuses (see Related work below).

Desired change

- Implement a scheduler command or module (intake_runner) that: queries Worklog for the highest-priority item in stage=idea, dispatches a non-interactive intake run ("do not ask questions"), records outcome and basic metrics, and logs errors for operator review.
- Include unit and integration tests for selection, dispatch, error handling and outcome detection.
- Update documentation and add an implementation README describing operational parameters and configuration (poll interval, timeouts).

Related work

- AM-0MNU7MXP40060TW7 — Automated intake scheduled task (parent coordination & examples)
- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection (selection logic and tests)
- AM-0MNUB5351000HNC9 — Intake completion detection (detection of intake_complete / input_needed)
- AM-0MNUB5S92008T1QG — Intake error handling and retry
- AM-0MNUB5MH70032NL9 — Intake runner auto-registration

Potentially related docs

- src/ampa/scheduler_schema.md — scheduler schema and existing cron-like definitions (useful for integration)
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md — operator guidance for input_needed status and triage

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNU7MXP40060TW7 — Automated intake scheduled task: Parent coordination item that collects patterns for selection, dispatch and assign workflows; provides examples and higher-level orchestration guidance.
- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection: Implementation and unit tests for selecting a candidate from stage=idea; reuse selection predicates and tests where practical.
- AM-0MNUB5351000HNC9 — Intake completion detection: Detection logic and metrics for intake_complete vs input_needed; needed for recording outcome and metrics in SchedulerStore.
- AM-0MNUB5S92008T1QG — Intake error handling and retry: Retry/backoff guidelines and operator alerting patterns; relevant for robust dispatch.
- AM-0MNUB5YR0002EALZ — Intake metrics and observability: Observability recommendations (metrics names, stores) to help ensure the intake-runner emits consistent metrics.

Each item above was discovered via Worklog search for "intake" and the repository scheduler docs; include links to these items in the PR description when implementing.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "Should the intake-runner process multiple items per run or only one?"
  - Answer (agent inference): The seed and existing work maintain the constraint: one item per run. Source: work item descriptions and Automated intake Scheduled Task artifacts found in repo. Final: yes.

- Q: "May the intake-runner interactively ask the requester for missing info?"
  - Answer (user directive): No. The calling invocation specified "do not ask questions". Source: original seed context passed to this intake and repository intake conventions. Final: no.

- OPEN QUESTION: "Are there target environments or runtime constraints for the intake-runner scheduler (e.g., deploy to cloud scheduler vs. in-repo cron)?"
  - Answer: OPEN QUESTION — no authoritative answer found in repository; affects deployment and operational monitoring. Directed to operators/maintainers.

Files to create or update (implementation checklist)

- src/ampa/scheduler/intake_runner.py — main implementation
- tests/unit/test_intake_selector.py — unit tests for selection logic
- tests/integration/test_intake_dispatch.py — integration test for dispatch and outcome detection
- docs/operator-guide-intake-runner.md — operational docs and configuration

Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Scope creep — implement only selection, dispatch, detection and basic assignment; record additional features as separate work items. Mitigation: create follow-up tasks for extra features.
- Risk: Dispatch failures could leave items in an unknown state. Mitigation: implement idempotent dispatch and a retry policy; surface failures to operators.
- Assumption: Worklog supports an "input_needed" status (existing work items indicate this has been added). If not available, the runner must fallback to adding a comment and tagging the work item.

## Summary
Implement robust error handling and retry logic with backoff to prevent infinite loops and spam.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Max 3 retry attempts per work item
- Exponential backoff between retries (15min, 30min, 60min)
- Track retry count in scheduler store
- Escalate to notification after max retries

## Minimal Implementation
- Add retry tracking to scheduler state
- Implement backoff calculation
- Add retry limit enforcement
- Discord notification on permanent failure

Headline: Intake brief for W1 (do not ask questions)

Problem statement

The intake was requested for work item W1 with the explicit instruction: "do not ask questions". The automation must produce a clear intake brief that either: (a) confirms the item has sufficient information to proceed, or (b) records missing information as OPEN QUESTIONS without prompting the requester.

Users

- Requester: person or automation that submitted W1 and expects non-interactive intake processing.
- Operator: AMPA operators and maintainers who will triage or act on items flagged by automation.
- Engineers: implementers who will convert the intake into a plan or PR.

Success criteria

- The work item description contains a clear Problem statement and at least one implementable Acceptance Criterion. (Measurable: includes an "Acceptance Criteria" section.)
- If information is missing, the work item is set to status=input_needed and contains an Appendix with OPEN QUESTIONS. (Measurable: presence of an "Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers" section.)
- If sufficient information exists, the work item is set to stage=intake_complete and its description contains the intake draft file reference. (Measurable: stage == intake_complete and description includes the draft filename.)
- All related documentation is updated to reflect the change, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

Constraints

- Work item AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU exists with title "Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)" and is assigned to Map at stage=idea status=in-progress.

Desired change

- Produce a finalized intake brief appended to the work item description. If missing information prevents progress, set status=input_needed and record OPEN QUESTIONS.

Related work

- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — W1 (earlier intake instance)

Problem statement

The intake was requested for work item W1 with the explicit instruction: "do not ask questions". The seed contains no technical details or acceptance criteria. The automation must produce a concise intake brief that either confirms the item is implementable as-is, or records missing information as OPEN QUESTIONS without interacting with the requester.

Users

- Requester: person or automation that submitted W1 and expects non-interactive intake processing.
- Operator: AMPA operators and maintainers who will triage or act on items flagged by automation.
- Engineers: implementers who will convert the intake into a plan or PR.

User stories

- As an engineer, I need a clear problem statement and measurable acceptance criteria so I can implement and test W1.
- As an operator, I want missing information recorded as OPEN QUESTIONS so I can follow up without changing the "do not ask questions" directive.

Success criteria

1. The work item description contains a clear Problem statement and at least one implementable Acceptance Criterion. (Measurable: description includes an "Acceptance Criteria" section with >=1 bullets.)
2. If information is missing, the work item is set to status=input_needed and contains explicit OPEN QUESTIONS recorded in the Appendix. (Measurable: description or comments contain an "Appendix: Clarifying questions" section.)
3. If sufficient information exists, the work item stage is set to intake_complete and the description contains the finalized brief. (Measurable: stage == intake_complete and description references this draft filename.)
4. All related documentation is updated to reflect the change, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
5. Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

Acceptance Criteria

- At least one measurable acceptance criterion is present in the work item description. (e.g. "Feature X accepts inputs A/B and returns C within 200ms; unit tests covering cases X/Y/Z exist")
- If information is missing, an "Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers" section records OPEN QUESTIONS and the work item status is set to input_needed.
- All related documentation is updated to reflect the change, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- Full project test suite passes after changes are applied.

Constraints

- Preserve the instruction "do not ask questions": do not prompt the requester for clarification. Record any missing info as OPEN QUESTIONS.
- Do not close the work item; do not change ownership.
- Follow repository conventions and do not include files excluded by .gitignore in the Appendix.

Existing state

- Work item AM-0MNVMQMN1008JTSB exists with title "W1: do not ask questions" and is assigned to Map at stage=idea status=in-progress.
- The seed text contains only: "W1 do not ask questions" with no additional acceptance criteria or design notes.

Desired change

- Produce a short intake brief capturing the intent of the seed. If details are missing, record OPEN QUESTIONS and set status=input_needed; otherwise, set stage=intake_complete and update description with this draft.

Related work

- .worklog entries that reference similar automated intakes (search term: "do not ask questions"). See work items: AM-0MNVH3LC10089RVL (W1 do not ask questions), AM-0MNV1EYGY004VBIY (W1: Do not ask questions), AM-0MNV13OX2004DLSH (AM-0MNUB5S92008T1QG). These are likely duplicates or related automation runs.

Work items:

- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — "W1": An earlier/open intake titled W1 created from operator input; shares the same short identifier and intent.

- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — "Intake query and selection": A completed item whose comments reference multiple W1 intakes; useful for context about intake selection and previous decisions.

data.description:
Problem statement
-----------------
W1: "do not ask questions" is an intake representing an automated request to run the repository intake process without prompting the requester for follow-up clarifications. The intake must capture enough context to allow implementers to continue to planning and implementation without further clarification.

Users
-----
- Operators and automation running the intake selector (e.g., AMPA scheduler) who need an intake command that can be executed unattended.
- Developers and product managers who will consume the work item to create implementation plans and PRs.

User stories
-----------
- As an operator, I want to dispatch intake runs with the directive "do not ask questions" so the automation performs intake processing without creating input_needed states or prompting the requester.
- As a developer, I want the work item to contain measurable success criteria and links to related artifacts so I can implement changes without needing further clarifications.

Success criteria
----------------
- Intake draft exists in the repository and is attached to the work item description. (verified)
- Work item status is set to "open" and stage is "intake_complete" when the draft is finalised. (verified by wl status)
- Any automated intake runner consumes the directive and does not set status=input_needed during the automated run, unless an explicit blocking error occurs. (testable via integration test)
- All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

Constraints
-----------
- The intake directive must not suppress required safety checks or necessary clarifying questions that would otherwise prevent a correct implementation.
- Automation must preserve traceability: any suppressed questions or skipped clarifications must be recorded on the work item as an Appendix entry.  
- Respect existing project automation rules: do not modify scheduler behaviour outside this work item.

Existing state
--------------
- The repository already contains intake automation code (src/ampa/intake_runner.py, src/ampa/intake_selector.py, src/ampa/engine/dispatch.py) that uses the phrase "do not ask questions" in canonical commands.
- Multiple prior work items and tests reference W1 and the "do not ask questions" directive (see Related work below).

Desired change
--------------
- Formalise an intake brief for W1 that documents intent, success criteria, constraints, risks, and related work.  
- Ensure the intake automation interprets this directive consistently and provides required traceability (Appendix of questions answered or suppressed).

Risks & assumptions
--------------------
- Risk: Automation suppresses clarifying questions and a critical missing detail causes incorrect behaviour. Mitigation: The runner must set status=input_needed for blocking errors and record suppressed clarifications in the Appendix.
- Risk: Scheduler integration gaps (auto-registration, rate-limiting) could cause unexpected dispatch timing or duplicate runs. Mitigation: Verify auto-registration and retries tasks (`AM-0MNUB5MH70032NL9`, `AM-0MNUB5S92008T1QG`) before enabling unattended wide-scale runs.
- Assumption: The "do not ask questions" directive is intended to avoid requester prompts but not to bypass safety checks or required approvals. If this assumption is incorrect, clarify scope. (OPEN QUESTION)
- Scope-scope mitigation: Record any additional feature requests or refactors discovered during work as linked Worklog items rather than expanding this item's scope.

Related work
------------
- src/ampa/engine/dispatch.py — constructs canonical intake command using "do not ask questions" and calls into process dispatch.
- src/ampa/intake_runner.py — intake runner that records dispatch outcomes and may set status=input_needed.
- src/ampa/intake_selector.py — selection logic for intake candidates.
- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection (completed) referencing earlier draft material.
- AM-0MNVP4MRS006C1L7 — W1: do not ask questions (current work item created by this intake run)

Related work (automated report)

Problem statement

The intake was requested for work item "W1" with the explicit instruction "do not ask questions". The seed contains no technical details, acceptance criteria, or desired outcome. The automation must produce a concise intake brief that either confirms the item is implementable as-is or records missing information as OPEN QUESTIONS for later follow-up.

Users

- Requester: person or automation that submitted W1 and expects non-interactive intake processing.
- Operators / Maintainers: engineers and triage staff who will act on OPEN QUESTIONS recorded by the automation.
- Implementers: engineers who will convert the intake into an implementation plan or PR.

Success criteria

- The work item description contains a clear Problem statement and at least one implementable Acceptance Criterion. (Measurable: description includes an "Acceptance Criteria" section and length > 20 characters.)
- If information is missing, the work item is set to status=input_needed and contains an Appendix with OPEN QUESTIONS. (Measurable: description or comments include an "Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers" section.)

- All related documentation is updated to reflect the change, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

Constraints

- Do not ask interactive questions; record missing information as OPEN QUESTIONS.
- Preserve the work item's assignee and do not close the work item during intake.
- Do not include or quote files excluded by .gitignore in the Appendix.

Existing state

- Work item created with title and description equal to the seed input: "W1 do not ask questions". No additional context exists in the work item.

Desired change

- Draft a concise intake brief containing a problem statement, users, success criteria, constraints, existing state, desired change, related work, and an Appendix of clarifying questions. If the item is implementable as-is, mark stage=intake_complete; otherwise record OPEN QUESTIONS and set status=input_needed.

data.description:
Headline: Produce a concise, non-interactive intake brief for W1 that either makes the item implementable or records OPEN QUESTIONS for operators.

Problem statement

The seed input is the string "W1 do not ask questions" with an explicit directive not to contact the requester. No technical details, desired outcome, or acceptance criteria were provided. The automation must produce a concise intake brief that either:
- confirms the item is implementable now with measurable Acceptance Criteria, or
- records missing information as OPEN QUESTIONS in an Appendix for operator follow-up (without contacting the requester).

Users

- Requester: the person/automation that created W1 and requested non-interactive intake.
- Operators / Maintainers: triage OPEN QUESTIONS and supply missing details.
- Engineers: implement the work once Acceptance Criteria are present.

User stories

- As the requester, I want W1 to contain clear requirements so work can start without Q&A.
- As an engineer, I need measurable Acceptance Criteria and constraints to implement and test the change.

Success criteria

1. The work item includes a clear Problem statement and at least one implementable Acceptance Criterion (an "Acceptance Criteria" section with >=1 bullet).
2. If details are missing, the work item is set to `status=input_needed` and includes an Appendix with explicit OPEN QUESTIONS.
3. If details are sufficient, the work item is set to `stage=intake_complete` and the finalized brief is published (description references this draft filename).
4. Related documentation is updated as needed (code comments, README, docs).
5. The full project test suite passes after changes.

Acceptance Criteria

- A1: At least one implementable, testable Acceptance Criterion is present (e.g., input/output, behaviour, UI change).
- A2: If insufficient detail exists, set `status=input_needed` and list OPEN QUESTIONS in the Appendix.

Constraints

- Do not ask the requester questions; record missing details as OPEN QUESTIONS for operators.
- Preserve the work item's assignee and do not close the item during intake.
- Follow repo conventions; do not include files ignored by .gitignore in the Appendix.

Existing state

- Seed input: "W1 do not ask questions". No other context.
- Several prior drafts and related work items exist; listed in Related work below.

Desired change

- Produce a concise intake brief with problem, users, measurable Acceptance Criteria, constraints, and related work. If details are missing, add OPEN QUESTIONS in the Appendix and set `status=input_needed`.

Related work

- AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU — earlier draft preserving the non-interactive directive.
- AM-0MNVH3LC10089RVL — prior intake with effort/risk notes.
- AM-0MNVNAJJB004WMJD — more complete draft with related-file references.
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md, docs/developer-guide-status-integration.md — guidance for `input_needed`.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "Who are the primary users and stakeholders for W1?" — Answer: OPEN QUESTION (seed input).
- Q: "What is the desired outcome or behaviour for W1 (feature, bug-fix, refactor)?" — Answer: OPEN QUESTION (seed input).
- Q: "Are there any Acceptance Criteria or tests already defined for W1?" — Answer: OPEN QUESTION (seed input).

Notes

- This draft preserves the "do not ask questions" directive by recording missing details as OPEN QUESTIONS for operators to triage.

Acceptance Criteria (operational)

- When details exist: include at least one concrete, testable Acceptance Criterion using a Given / When / Then template.
- When details are missing: include OPEN QUESTIONS in the Appendix and set `status=input_needed` so operators can follow up.

Worklog commands (copy-paste ready)

- Mark item input_needed: `wl update <id> --status input_needed`
- Mark item intake_complete: `wl update <id> --stage intake_complete`

Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Missing or ambiguous requirements — mitigation: set `status=input_needed` and require operator confirmation before implementation.
- Risk: Duplicate work — mitigation: check and link related items; prefer updating an existing item.
- Risk: Scope creep — mitigation: do not expand scope here; create linked work items for extra features.
- Assumption: Operators will triage OPEN QUESTIONS; implementers will not start until Acceptance Criteria exist or the item is `intake_complete`.

Suggested next steps (operator-facing)

1. If the intake should be actionable now, add at least one implementable Acceptance Criterion under "Acceptance Criteria", then run:
   - `wl update <id> --stage intake_complete`
2. To preserve the non-interactive constraint and await details, run:
   - `wl update <id> --status input_needed`
3. Optionally link any related work items in the description or comments for traceability.

Report

Automated related-work search (conservative filter). Low-relevance matches and incidental logs were omitted.
