      "id": "AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU",
      "title": "Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)",
      "description": "Headline: Intake brief for W1 (do not ask questions)

Problem statement

The intake was requested for work item W1 with the explicit instruction: \"do not ask questions\". The automation must produce a clear intake brief that either: (a) confirms the item has sufficient information to proceed, or (b) records missing information as OPEN QUESTIONS without prompting the requester.

Users

- Requester: person or automation that submitted W1 and expects non-interactive intake processing.
- Operator: AMPA operators and maintainers who will triage or act on items flagged by automation.
- Engineers: implementers who will convert the intake into a plan or PR.

Success criteria

- The work item description contains a clear Problem statement and at least one implementable Acceptance Criterion. (Measurable: includes an \"Acceptance Criteria\" section.)
- If information is missing, the work item is set to status=input_needed and contains an Appendix with OPEN QUESTIONS. (Measurable: presence of an \"Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers\" section.)
- If sufficient information exists, the work item is set to stage=intake_complete and its description contains the intake draft file reference. (Measurable: stage == intake_complete and description includes the draft filename.)
- All related documentation is updated to reflect the change, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

Constraints

- Do not ask interactive questions; record missing info as OPEN QUESTIONS.
- Do not close the work item; do not change ownership.
- Follow repository conventions and do not include files excluded by .gitignore in the Appendix.

Existing state

- Work item AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU exists with title \"Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)\" and is assigned to Map at stage=idea status=in-progress.

Desired change

- Produce a finalized intake brief appended to the work item description. If missing information prevents progress, set status=input_needed and record OPEN QUESTIONS.

Related work

- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — W1 (earlier intake instance)
- AM-0MNV1KR6Z0071HHR — W1 (prior intake)
- AM-0MNV1KTRC000PK06 — W1 (prior intake by agent)
- AM-0MNV1EYGY004VBIY — W1: Do not ask questions (explicit preserve of the directive)
- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection (selector/dispatch core)
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md
- docs/developer-guide-status-integration.md

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: \"Should the intake agent ask clarifying questions interactively?\" — Answer (seed intent): \"No; do not ask questions.\" Final: no interactive questions.
- Q: \"What is the canonical work item id to use for this intake?\" — Answer (agent inference): \"W1\" mapped to AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU. Final: AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU.
- Q: \"Are measurable acceptance criteria provided?\" — Answer (agent inference): Not present in seed. Final: OPEN QUESTION - requester must supply or accept proposed criteria.


Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Scope creep if unstated requirements are assumed. Mitigation: record additional requests as linked work items.
- Assumption: Requester will respond if status=input_needed is set.

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection: selector logic that chose W1 for intake; useful to trace selection rationale.
- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — Prior W1 intake: earlier run of the same non-interactive intake; useful precedent for handling missing acceptance criteria.
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md — Describes operator behaviour for items flagged input_needed; relevant policy for this intake.

# AMPA Audit Result

## Summary

The intake selector and runner correctly implement deterministic, single-item selection from idea-stage work items and include focused unit tests for selection, tie‑breaking, and JSON error handling. Remaining gaps block closure: there is no scheduler‑wide/global rate limiter, documentation is limited to a module docstring (no feature-level repo docs), and there is no recorded full‑project test‑suite green run verifying integration. Until those gaps are addressed the work item should remain open.

      "description": "# Intake Dispatch Mechanism

## Summary
Spawn opencode `/intake` sessions using OpenCodeRunDispatcher with appropriate command and timeout handling.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dispatches `opencode run \"/intake <id> Do not ask any questions...\"` command
- Uses existing OpenCodeRunDispatcher from engine.dispatch
- Configurable timeout (default: 1 hour)
- Captures dispatch result and work item ID for tracking

## Minimal Implementation
- Create `IntakeDispatcher` class
- Integrate with OpenCodeRunDispatcher
- Add timeout configuration via env var
- Error handling for dispatch failures

## Dependencies
- Feature: Intake query and selection (for work item selection)
- scheduler infrastructure

## Deliverables
- Code, tests, logging

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers
- Q: Should the intake task process multiple items per run, or one at a time?
  - Answer: One item at a time (to avoid overwhelming opencode sessions)
- Q: For agent auto-assignment, which approach for the first iteration?
  - Answer: Option B: Tag-based lookup in workflow descriptor
- Q: Should the intake-runner command be auto-registered like wl-audit and pr-monitor?
  - Answer: Auto-registered (like wl-audit and pr-monitor)",

      "id": "AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU",
      "title": "Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)",
      "description": "Headline: Intake brief for W1 (do not ask questions)

Problem statement

The intake was requested for work item W1 with the explicit instruction: \"do not ask questions\". The automation must produce a clear intake brief that either: (a) confirms the item has sufficient information to proceed, or (b) records missing information as OPEN QUESTIONS without prompting the requester.

Users

- Requester: person or automation that submitted W1 and expects non-interactive intake processing.
- Operator: AMPA operators and maintainers who will triage or act on items flagged by automation.
- Engineers: implementers who will convert the intake into a plan or PR.

Success criteria

- The work item description contains a clear Problem statement and at least one implementable Acceptance Criterion. (Measurable: includes an \"Acceptance Criteria\" section.)
- If information is missing, the work item is set to status=input_needed and contains an Appendix with OPEN QUESTIONS. (Measurable: presence of an \"Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers\" section.)
- If sufficient information exists, the work item is set to stage=intake_complete and its description contains the intake draft file reference. (Measurable: stage == intake_complete and description includes the draft filename.)
- All related documentation is updated to reflect the change, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

Constraints

- Do not ask interactive questions; record missing info as OPEN QUESTIONS.
- Do not close the work item; do not change ownership.
- Follow repository conventions and do not include files excluded by .gitignore in the Appendix.

Existing state

- Work item AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU exists with title \"Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)\" and is assigned to Map at stage=idea status=in-progress.

Desired change

- Produce a finalized intake brief appended to the work item description. If missing information prevents progress, set status=input_needed and record OPEN QUESTIONS.

Related work

- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — W1 (earlier intake instance)
- AM-0MNV1KR6Z0071HHR — W1 (prior intake)
- AM-0MNV1KTRC000PK06 — W1 (prior intake by agent)
- AM-0MNV1EYGY004VBIY — W1: Do not ask questions (explicit preserve of the directive)
- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection (selector/dispatch core)
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md
- docs/developer-guide-status-integration.md

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: \"Should the intake agent ask clarifying questions interactively?\" — Answer (seed intent): \"No; do not ask questions.\" Final: no interactive questions.
- Q: \"What is the canonical work item id to use for this intake?\" — Answer (agent inference): \"W1\" mapped to AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU. Final: AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU.
- Q: \"Are measurable acceptance criteria provided?\" — Answer (agent inference): Not present in seed. Final: OPEN QUESTION - requester must supply or accept proposed criteria.


Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Scope creep if unstated requirements are assumed. Mitigation: record additional requests as linked work items.
- Assumption: Requester will respond if status=input_needed is set.

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection: selector logic that chose W1 for intake; useful to trace selection rationale.
- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — Prior W1 intake: earlier run of the same non-interactive intake; useful precedent for handling missing acceptance criteria.
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md — Describes operator behaviour for items flagged input_needed; relevant policy for this intake.

# AMPA Audit Result

## Summary

The intake selector and runner correctly implement deterministic, single-item selection from idea-stage work items and include focused unit tests for selection, tie-breaking, and JSON error handling. Remaining gaps block closure: there is no scheduler-wide/global rate limiter (only per-item retry/backoff), documentation is limited to a module docstring and README (no feature-level docs), and there is no recorded full-project test-suite green run verifying integration. Until those gaps are addressed the work item should remain open.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

|---|-----------|---------|----------|

      "title": "Intake dispatch mechanism",
      "description": "# Intake Dispatch Mechanism

## Summary
Spawn opencode `/intake` sessions using OpenCodeRunDispatcher with appropriate command and timeout handling.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dispatches `opencode run \"/intake <id> Do not ask any questions...\"` command
- Uses existing OpenCodeRunDispatcher from engine.dispatch
- Configurable timeout (default: 1 hour)
- Captures dispatch result and work item ID for tracking

## Minimal Implementation
- Create `IntakeDispatcher` class
- Integrate with OpenCodeRunDispatcher
- Add timeout configuration via env var
- Error handling for dispatch failures

## Dependencies
- Feature: Intake query and selection (for work item selection)
- scheduler infrastructure

## Deliverables
- Code, tests, logging

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers
- Q: Should the intake task process multiple items per run, or one at a time?
  - Answer: One item at a time (to avoid overwhelming opencode sessions)
- Q: For agent auto-assignment, which approach for the first iteration?
  - Answer: Option B: Tag-based lookup in workflow descriptor
- Q: Should the intake-runner command be auto-registered like wl-audit and pr-monitor?
  - Answer: Auto-registered (like wl-audit and pr-monitor)",

      "title": "Tag-based agent assignment",
      "description": "# Tag-Based Agent Assignment

## Summary
Implement agent assignment logic using tag-based lookup in workflow descriptor.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Workflow descriptor defines tag→agent mappings
- Falls back to type-based assignment when no tags match
- Handles \"no matching agent\" case gracefully (leave unassigned)
- Respects agent availability/workload (basic check)

## Minimal Implementation
- Add `agentMappings` section to workflow descriptor schema
- Create `AgentAssigner` class with tag matching logic
- Update work item via `wl update <id> --assignee <agent>`
- Unit tests for assignment logic

## Dependencies
- Feature: Intake completion detection
- workflow descriptor

## Deliverables
- Code, tests, workflow descriptor example

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers
- Q: Should the intake task process multiple items per run, or one at a time?
  - Answer: One item at a time (to avoid overwhelming opencode sessions)
- Q: For agent auto-assignment, which approach for the first iteration?
  - Answer: Option B: Tag-based lookup in workflow descriptor
- Q: Should the intake-runner command be auto-registered like wl-audit and pr-monitor?
  - Answer: Auto-registered (like wl-audit and pr-monitor)",

      "id": "AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU",
      "title": "Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)",
      "description": "Headline: Intake brief for W1 (do not ask questions)

Problem statement

The intake was requested for work item W1 with the explicit instruction: \"do not ask questions\". The automation must produce a clear intake brief that either: (a) confirms the item has sufficient information to proceed, or (b) records missing information as OPEN QUESTIONS without prompting the requester.

Users

- Requester: person or automation that submitted W1 and expects non-interactive intake processing.
- Operator: AMPA operators and maintainers who will triage or act on items flagged by automation.
- Engineers: implementers who will convert the intake into a plan or PR.

Success criteria

- The work item description contains a clear Problem statement and at least one implementable Acceptance Criterion. (Measurable: includes an \"Acceptance Criteria\" section.)
- If information is missing, the work item is set to status=input_needed and contains an Appendix with OPEN QUESTIONS. (Measurable: presence of an \"Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers\" section.)
- If sufficient information exists, the work item is set to stage=intake_complete and its description contains the intake draft file reference. (Measurable: stage == intake_complete and description includes the draft filename.)
- All related documentation is updated to reflect the change, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

Constraints

- Do not ask interactive questions; record missing info as OPEN QUESTIONS.
- Do not close the work item; do not change ownership.
- Follow repository conventions and do not include files excluded by .gitignore in the Appendix.

Existing state

- Work item AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU exists with title \"Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)\" and is assigned to Map at stage=idea status=in-progress.

Desired change

- Produce a finalized intake brief appended to the work item description. If missing information prevents progress, set status=input_needed and record OPEN QUESTIONS.

Related work

- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — W1 (earlier intake instance)
- AM-0MNV1KR6Z0071HHR — W1 (prior intake)
- AM-0MNV1KTRC000PK06 — W1 (prior intake by agent)
- AM-0MNV1EYGY004VBIY — W1: Do not ask questions (explicit preserve of the directive)
- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection (selector/dispatch core)
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md
- docs/developer-guide-status-integration.md

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: \"Should the intake agent ask clarifying questions interactively?\" — Answer (seed intent): \"No; do not ask questions.\" Final: no interactive questions.
- Q: \"What is the canonical work item id to use for this intake?\" — Answer (agent inference): \"W1\" mapped to AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU. Final: AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU.
- Q: \"Are measurable acceptance criteria provided?\" — Answer (agent inference): Not present in seed. Final: OPEN QUESTION - requester must supply or accept proposed criteria.


Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Scope creep if unstated requirements are assumed. Mitigation: record additional requests as linked work items.
- Assumption: Requester will respond if status=input_needed is set.

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection: selector logic that chose W1 for intake; useful to trace selection rationale.
- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — Prior W1 intake: earlier run of the same non-interactive intake; useful precedent for handling missing acceptance criteria.
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md — Describes operator behaviour for items flagged input_needed; relevant policy for this intake.

data.comment:
# AMPA Audit Result

## Summary

The intake selector and runner correctly implement deterministic single-item selection from idea-stage work items and include focused unit tests for selection and edge cases. Remaining gaps prevent closure: there is no scheduler-wide/global rate limiter (only per-item retry/backoff), documentation is limited to a module docstring and general README entries (no feature-level docs), and there's no recorded full-project test-suite green run verifying integration.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | Query returns items with stage="idea" sorted by sortIndex descending (highest priority first) | met | src/ampa/intake_selector.py:44-46,95-117 — runs {

    {
      "id": "AM-0MNUB4V80006OGMK",
      "title": "Intake dispatch mechanism",
      "description": "# Intake Dispatch Mechanism

## Summary
Spawn opencode `/intake` sessions using OpenCodeRunDispatcher with appropriate command and timeout handling.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dispatches `opencode run \"/intake <id> Do not ask any questions...\"` command
- Uses existing OpenCodeRunDispatcher from engine.dispatch
- Configurable timeout (default: 1 hour)
- Captures dispatch result and work item ID for tracking

## Minimal Implementation
- Create `IntakeDispatcher` class
- Integrate with OpenCodeRunDispatcher
- Add timeout configuration via env var
- Error handling for dispatch failures

## Dependencies
- Feature: Intake query and selection (for work item selection)
- scheduler infrastructure

## Deliverables
- Code, tests, logging

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers
- Q: Should the intake task process multiple items per run, or one at a time?

    {
      "id": "AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU",
      "title": "Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)",
      "description": "Headline: Intake brief for W1 (do not ask questions)

Problem statement

The intake was requested for work item W1 with the explicit instruction: \"do not ask questions\". The automation must produce a clear intake brief that either: (a) confirms the item has sufficient information to proceed, or (b) records missing information as OPEN QUESTIONS without prompting the requester.

Users

- Requester: person or automation that submitted W1 and expects non-interactive intake processing.
- Operator: AMPA operators and maintainers who will triage or act on items flagged by automation.
- Engineers: implementers who will convert the intake into a plan or PR.

Success criteria

- The work item description contains a clear Problem statement and at least one implementable Acceptance Criterion. (Measurable: includes an \"Acceptance Criteria\" section.)
- If information is missing, the work item is set to status=input_needed and contains an Appendix with OPEN QUESTIONS. (Measurable: presence of an \"Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers\" section.)
- If sufficient information exists, the work item is set to stage=intake_complete and its description contains the intake draft file reference. (Measurable: stage == intake_complete and description includes the draft filename.)
- All related documentation is updated to reflect the change, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

Constraints

- Do not ask interactive questions; record missing info as OPEN QUESTIONS.
- Do not close the work item; do not change ownership.
- Follow repository conventions and do not include files excluded by .gitignore in the Appendix.

Existing state

- Work item AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU exists with title \"Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)\" and is assigned to Map at stage=idea status=in-progress.

Desired change

- Produce a finalized intake brief appended to the work item description. If missing information prevents progress, set status=input_needed and record OPEN QUESTIONS.

Related work

- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — W1 (earlier intake instance)
- AM-0MNV1KR6Z0071HHR — W1 (prior intake)
- AM-0MNV1KTRC000PK06 — W1 (prior intake by agent)
- AM-0MNV1EYGY004VBIY — W1: Do not ask questions (explicit preserve of the directive)
- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection (selector/dispatch core)
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md
- docs/developer-guide-status-integration.md

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: \"Should the intake agent ask clarifying questions interactively?\" — Answer (seed intent): \"No; do not ask questions.\" Final: no interactive questions.
- Q: \"What is the canonical work item id to use for this intake?\" — Answer (agent inference): \"W1\" mapped to AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU. Final: AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU.
- Q: \"Are measurable acceptance criteria provided?\" — Answer (agent inference): Not present in seed. Final: OPEN QUESTION - requester must supply or accept proposed criteria.


Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Scope creep if unstated requirements are assumed. Mitigation: record additional requests as linked work items.
- Assumption: Requester will respond if status=input_needed is set.

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection: selector logic that chose W1 for intake; useful to trace selection rationale.
- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — Prior W1 intake: earlier run of the same non-interactive intake; useful precedent for handling missing acceptance criteria.
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md — Describes operator behaviour for items flagged input_needed; relevant policy for this intake.

data.comment:
# AMPA Audit Result

## Summary

The intake query/selection component reliably queries Worklog, normalizes responses, and deterministically selects a single highest-priority "idea"-stage item. Selection and edge-case unit tests exist. Remaining gaps prevent closing: partial documentation and no recorded full-project green test run associated with these changes.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | Query returns items with stage="idea" sorted by sortIndex descending (highest priority first) | met | src/ampa/intake_selector.py:95-117 — _item_sort_key implements (-sortIndex, -updated_ts, id) and query_candidates parses wl output. |
| 2 | Selects single highest priority item per run (one at a time to avoid overwhelming opencode sessions) | met | src/ampa/intake_selector.py:119-127 & src/ampa/intake_runner.py:48 — select_top returns top candidate and runner calls it. |
| 3 | Handles empty result set gracefully (no-op, no errors) | met | src/ampa/intake_runner.py:44-46 & tests/test_intake_selector.py:22-26 — returns no selection for empty candidate lists. |
| 4 | Respects scheduler global rate limiting | met | src/ampa/scheduler.py:326-334 — Scheduler implements _global_rate_limited() and uses it in select_next (call at line 352) so scheduled runs respect global cooldown. |
| 5 | Unit tests cover selection logic and edge cases | met | tests/test_intake_selector.py:22-69 — tests for empty response, wrapped responses, tiebreaks, and invalid JSON. |
| 6 | All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries | partial | src/ampa/intake_selector.py:1-10 — only a module-level docstring and a draft exist; no feature-level published README/docs page found. |
| 7 | Full project test suite must pass with the new changes | unmet | .worklog/logs/sync.log:1031 — recent sync / CI records show test failures; no verified full-project green run recorded for these changes. |

## Recommendation

Can this item be closed? No.

      "description": "# Intake Dispatch Mechanism

- Dispatches `opencode run \"/intake <id> Do not ask any questions...\"` command

- Configurable timeout (default: 1 hour)
- Captures dispatch result and work item ID for tracking

  - Answer: Option B: Tag-based lookup in workflow descriptor

      "id": "AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU",
      "title": "Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)",
      "description": "Headline: Intake brief for W1 (do not ask questions)

Problem statement

The intake was requested for work item W1 with the explicit instruction: \"do not ask questions\". The automation must produce a clear intake brief that either: (a) confirms the item has sufficient information to proceed, or (b) records missing information as OPEN QUESTIONS without prompting the requester.

Users

- Requester: person or automation that submitted W1 and expects non-interactive intake processing.
- Operator: AMPA operators and maintainers who will triage or act on items flagged by automation.
- Engineers: implementers who will convert the intake into a plan or PR.

Success criteria

- The work item description contains a clear Problem statement and at least one implementable Acceptance Criterion. (Measurable: includes an \"Acceptance Criteria\" section.)
- If information is missing, the work item is set to status=input_needed and contains an Appendix with OPEN QUESTIONS. (Measurable: presence of an \"Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers\" section.)
- If sufficient information exists, the work item is set to stage=intake_complete and its description contains the intake draft file reference. (Measurable: stage == intake_complete and description includes the draft filename.)
- All related documentation is updated to reflect the change, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

Constraints

- Do not ask interactive questions; record missing info as OPEN QUESTIONS.
- Do not close the work item; do not change ownership.
- Follow repository conventions and do not include files excluded by .gitignore in the Appendix.

Existing state

- Work item AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU exists with title \"Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)\" and is assigned to Map at stage=idea status=in-progress.

Desired change

- Produce a finalized intake brief appended to the work item description. If missing information prevents progress, set status=input_needed and record OPEN QUESTIONS.

Related work

- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — W1 (earlier intake instance)
- AM-0MNV1KR6Z0071HHR — W1 (prior intake)
- AM-0MNV1KTRC000PK06 — W1 (prior intake by agent)
- AM-0MNV1EYGY004VBIY — W1: Do not ask questions (explicit preserve of the directive)
- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection (selector/dispatch core)
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md
- docs/developer-guide-status-integration.md

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: \"Should the intake agent ask clarifying questions interactively?\" — Answer (seed intent): \"No; do not ask questions.\" Final: no interactive questions.
- Q: \"What is the canonical work item id to use for this intake?\" — Answer (agent inference): \"W1\" mapped to AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU. Final: AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU.
- Q: \"Are measurable acceptance criteria provided?\" — Answer (agent inference): Not present in seed. Final: OPEN QUESTION - requester must supply or accept proposed criteria.


Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Scope creep if unstated requirements are assumed. Mitigation: record additional requests as linked work items.
- Assumption: Requester will respond if status=input_needed is set.

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection: selector logic that chose W1 for intake; useful to trace selection rationale.
- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — Prior W1 intake: earlier run of the same non-interactive intake; useful precedent for handling missing acceptance criteria.
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md — Describes operator behaviour for items flagged input_needed; relevant policy for this intake.

## Summary

The intake selector and runner correctly query Worklog for idea-stage items, deterministically sort by priority, and select a single highest-priority candidate.       "description": "# Intake Dispatch Mechanism

## Summary
Spawn opencode `/intake` sessions using OpenCodeRunDispatcher with appropriate command and timeout handling.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dispatches `opencode run \"/intake <id> Do not ask any questions...\"` command
- Uses existing OpenCodeRunDispatcher from engine.dispatch
- Configurable timeout (default: 1 hour)
- Captures dispatch result and work item ID for tracking

## Minimal Implementation
- Create `IntakeDispatcher` class
- Integrate with OpenCodeRunDispatcher
- Add timeout configuration via env var
- Error handling for dispatch failures

## Dependencies
- Feature: Intake query and selection (for work item selection)
- scheduler infrastructure

## Deliverables
- Code, tests, logging

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers
- Q: Should the intake task process multiple items per run, or one at a time?
  - Answer: One item at a time (to avoid overwhelming opencode sessions)
- Q: For agent auto-assignment, which approach for the first iteration?
  - Answer: Option B: Tag-based lookup in workflow descriptor
- Q: Should the intake-runner command be auto-registered like wl-audit and pr-monitor?
  - Answer: Auto-registered (like wl-audit and pr-monitor)",

      "id": "AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU",
      "title": "Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)",
      "description": "Headline: Intake brief for W1 (do not ask questions)

Problem statement

The intake was requested for work item W1 with the explicit instruction: \"do not ask questions\". The automation must produce a clear intake brief that either: (a) confirms the item has sufficient information to proceed, or (b) records missing information as OPEN QUESTIONS without prompting the requester.

Users

- Requester: person or automation that submitted W1 and expects non-interactive intake processing.
- Operator: AMPA operators and maintainers who will triage or act on items flagged by automation.
- Engineers: implementers who will convert the intake into a plan or PR.

Success criteria

- The work item description contains a clear Problem statement and at least one implementable Acceptance Criterion. (Measurable: includes an \"Acceptance Criteria\" section.)
- If information is missing, the work item is set to status=input_needed and contains an Appendix with OPEN QUESTIONS. (Measurable: presence of an \"Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers\" section.)
- If sufficient information exists, the work item is set to stage=intake_complete and its description contains the intake draft file reference. (Measurable: stage == intake_complete and description includes the draft filename.)
- All related documentation is updated to reflect the change, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

Constraints

- Do not ask interactive questions; record missing info as OPEN QUESTIONS.
- Do not close the work item; do not change ownership.
- Follow repository conventions and do not include files excluded by .gitignore in the Appendix.

Existing state

- Work item AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU exists with title \"Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)\" and is assigned to Map at stage=idea status=in-progress.

Desired change

- Produce a finalized intake brief appended to the work item description. If missing information prevents progress, set status=input_needed and record OPEN QUESTIONS.

Related work

- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — W1 (earlier intake instance)
- AM-0MNV1KR6Z0071HHR — W1 (prior intake)
- AM-0MNV1KTRC000PK06 — W1 (prior intake by agent)
- AM-0MNV1EYGY004VBIY — W1: Do not ask questions (explicit preserve of the directive)
- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection (selector/dispatch core)
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md
- docs/developer-guide-status-integration.md

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: \"Should the intake agent ask clarifying questions interactively?\" — Answer (seed intent): \"No; do not ask questions.\" Final: no interactive questions.
- Q: \"What is the canonical work item id to use for this intake?\" — Answer (agent inference): \"W1\" mapped to AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU. Final: AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU.
- Q: \"Are measurable acceptance criteria provided?\" — Answer (agent inference): Not present in seed. Final: OPEN QUESTION - requester must supply or accept proposed criteria.


Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Scope creep if unstated requirements are assumed. Mitigation: record additional requests as linked work items.
- Assumption: Requester will respond if status=input_needed is set.

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection: selector logic that chose W1 for intake; useful to trace selection rationale.
- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — Prior W1 intake: earlier run of the same non-interactive intake; useful precedent for handling missing acceptance criteria.
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md — Describes operator behaviour for items flagged input_needed; relevant policy for this intake.

      "title": "Intake dispatch mechanism",
      "description": "# Intake Dispatch Mechanism

## Summary
Spawn opencode `/intake` sessions using OpenCodeRunDispatcher with appropriate command and timeout handling.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dispatches `opencode run \"/intake <id> Do not ask any questions...\"` command
- Uses existing OpenCodeRunDispatcher from engine.dispatch
- Configurable timeout (default: 1 hour)
- Captures dispatch result and work item ID for tracking

## Minimal Implementation
- Create `IntakeDispatcher` class
- Integrate with OpenCodeRunDispatcher
- Add timeout configuration via env var
- Error handling for dispatch failures

## Dependencies
- Feature: Intake query and selection (for work item selection)
- scheduler infrastructure

## Deliverables
- Code, tests, logging

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers
- Q: Should the intake task process multiple items per run, or one at a time?
  - Answer: One item at a time (to avoid overwhelming opencode sessions)
- Q: For agent auto-assignment, which approach for the first iteration?
  - Answer: Option B: Tag-based lookup in workflow descriptor
- Q: Should the intake-runner command be auto-registered like wl-audit and pr-monitor?
  - Answer: Auto-registered (like wl-audit and pr-monitor)",

      "id": "AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU",
      "title": "Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)",
      "description": "Headline: Intake brief for W1 (do not ask questions)

Problem statement

The intake was requested for work item W1 with the explicit instruction: \"do not ask questions\". The automation must produce a clear intake brief that either: (a) confirms the item has sufficient information to proceed, or (b) records missing information as OPEN QUESTIONS without prompting the requester.

Users

- Requester: person or automation that submitted W1 and expects non-interactive intake processing.
- Operator: AMPA operators and maintainers who will triage or act on items flagged by automation.
- Engineers: implementers who will convert the intake into a plan or PR.

Success criteria

- The work item description contains a clear Problem statement and at least one implementable Acceptance Criterion. (Measurable: includes an \"Acceptance Criteria\" section.)
- If information is missing, the work item is set to status=input_needed and contains an Appendix with OPEN QUESTIONS. (Measurable: presence of an \"Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers\" section.)
- If sufficient information exists, the work item is set to stage=intake_complete and its description contains the intake draft file reference. (Measurable: stage == intake_complete and description includes the draft filename.)
- All related documentation is updated to reflect the change, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

Constraints

- Do not ask interactive questions; record missing info as OPEN QUESTIONS.
- Do not close the work item; do not change ownership.
- Follow repository conventions and do not include files excluded by .gitignore in the Appendix.

Existing state

- Work item AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU exists with title \"Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)\" and is assigned to Map at stage=idea status=in-progress.

Desired change

- Produce a finalized intake brief appended to the work item description. If missing information prevents progress, set status=input_needed and record OPEN QUESTIONS.

Related work

- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — W1 (earlier intake instance)
- AM-0MNV1KR6Z0071HHR — W1 (prior intake)
- AM-0MNV1KTRC000PK06 — W1 (prior intake by agent)
- AM-0MNV1EYGY004VBIY — W1: Do not ask questions (explicit preserve of the directive)
- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection (selector/dispatch core)
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md
- docs/developer-guide-status-integration.md

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: \"Should the intake agent ask clarifying questions interactively?\" — Answer (seed intent): \"No; do not ask questions.\" Final: no interactive questions.
- Q: \"What is the canonical work item id to use for this intake?\" — Answer (agent inference): \"W1\" mapped to AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU. Final: AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU.
- Q: \"Are measurable acceptance criteria provided?\" — Answer (agent inference): Not present in seed. Final: OPEN QUESTION - requester must supply or accept proposed criteria.


Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Scope creep if unstated requirements are assumed. Mitigation: record additional requests as linked work items.
- Assumption: Requester will respond if status=input_needed is set.

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection: selector logic that chose W1 for intake; useful to trace selection rationale.
- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — Prior W1 intake: earlier run of the same non-interactive intake; useful precedent for handling missing acceptance criteria.
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md — Describes operator behaviour for items flagged input_needed; relevant policy for this intake.

data.comment:
# AMPA Audit Result

## Summary

The intake selector and runner are implemented, unit-tested, and integrated with the scheduler. Selection and dispatch logic behave as specified for normal and edge cases. Documentation is thin (module docstring + incidental README mention) and there is no recorded full-project green test run for these changes, so the work item should not be closed.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | Query returns items with stage="idea" sorted by sortIndex descending (highest priority first) | met | src/ampa/intake_selector.py:95-117 — sort key uses (-sortIndex, -updated_ts, id) and sorting is applied. |
| 2 | Selects single highest priority item per run (one at a time to avoid overwhelming opencode sessions) | met | src/ampa/intake_runner.py:48-56 — calls selector.select_top(...) and processes a single selected id. |
| 3 | Handles empty result set gracefully (no-op, no errors) | met | src/ampa/intake_runner.py:44-46 — returns {"selected": None} when no candidates. |
| 4 | Respects scheduler global rate limiting | met | src/ampa/scheduler.py:350-353 — select_next checks _global_rate_limited(now) and returns None when global rate-limit applies. |
| 5 | Unit tests cover selection logic and edge cases | met | tests/test_intake_selector.py:22-69 — tests for empty, wrapped responses, tie-breaks, and invalid JSON are present. |
| 6 | All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries | partial | src/ampa/intake_selector.py:1-10 — only a module docstring exists; no dedicated feature-level documentation found. |
| 7 | Full project test suite must pass with the new changes | unmet | .github/workflows/ci.yml:93-95 — CI is configured to run the full test matrix, but work-item audit/comments indicate no recorded full-project green run for these changes. |

## Recommendation

Can this item be closed? No.

data.author: ampa-scheduler
data.comment:
# AMPA Audit Result

## Summary

The intake selector and runner correctly implement the query → sort → select flow and are integrated with the scheduler. Unit tests cover selector behaviors (empty lists, wrapped responses, tie-breaks, invalid JSON). Documentation coverage is minimal and there is no recorded successful full-project test run for these changes, so the work item should not be closed.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | Query returns items with stage="idea" sorted by sortIndex descending (highest priority first) | met | src/ampa/intake_selector.py:103-117 — constructs (-sortIndex, -timestamp, id) key and uses it for sorting. |
| 2 | Selects single highest priority item per run (one at a time to avoid overwhelming opencode sessions) | met | src/ampa/intake_selector.py:119-127 — select_top returns first sorted candidate; src/ampa/intake_runner.py:48-56 — runner uses selector.select_top and processes a single id. |
| 3 | Handles empty result set gracefully (no-op, no errors) | met | tests/test_intake_selector.py:22-26 — unit test asserts empty-list behavior; src/ampa/intake_runner.py:41-46 — returns {"selected": None} when no candidates. |
| 4 | Respects scheduler global rate limiting | met | src/ampa/scheduler.py:326-334 — _global_rate_limited used by select_next and prevents runs within global_min_interval. |
| 5 | Unit tests cover selection logic and edge cases | met | tests/test_intake_selector.py:22-69 — tests for empty, wrapped responses, tie-breaks, and invalid JSON present. |
| 6 | All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries | partial | src/ampa/intake_selector.py:1-10 — only a module-level docstring; README.md:137-141 — mentions related status but no dedicated feature-level intake documentation. |
| 7 | Full project test suite must pass with the new changes | unmet | .github/workflows/ci.yml:93-95 — CI runs pytest; audit/commentary on the work item indicate no recorded full-project green run for these changes. |

## Recommendation

Can this item be closed? No.

      "id": "AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU",
      "title": "Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)",
      "description": "Headline: Intake brief for W1 (do not ask questions)

Problem statement

The intake was requested for work item W1 with the explicit instruction: \"do not ask questions\". The automation must produce a clear intake brief that either: (a) confirms the item has sufficient information to proceed, or (b) records missing information as OPEN QUESTIONS without prompting the requester.

Users

- Requester: person or automation that submitted W1 and expects non-interactive intake processing.
- Operator: AMPA operators and maintainers who will triage or act on items flagged by automation.
- Engineers: implementers who will convert the intake into a plan or PR.

Success criteria

- The work item description contains a clear Problem statement and at least one implementable Acceptance Criterion. (Measurable: includes an \"Acceptance Criteria\" section.)
- If information is missing, the work item is set to status=input_needed and contains an Appendix with OPEN QUESTIONS. (Measurable: presence of an \"Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers\" section.)
- If sufficient information exists, the work item is set to stage=intake_complete and its description contains the intake draft file reference. (Measurable: stage == intake_complete and description includes the draft filename.)
- All related documentation is updated to reflect the change, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

Constraints

- Do not ask interactive questions; record missing info as OPEN QUESTIONS.
- Do not close the work item; do not change ownership.
- Follow repository conventions and do not include files excluded by .gitignore in the Appendix.

Existing state

- Work item AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU exists with title \"Intake: W1 (do not ask questions)\" and is assigned to Map at stage=idea status=in-progress.

Desired change

- Produce a finalized intake brief appended to the work item description. If missing information prevents progress, set status=input_needed and record OPEN QUESTIONS.

Related work

- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — W1 (earlier intake instance)
- AM-0MNV1KR6Z0071HHR — W1 (prior intake)
- AM-0MNV1KTRC000PK06 — W1 (prior intake by agent)
- AM-0MNV1EYGY004VBIY — W1: Do not ask questions (explicit preserve of the directive)
- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection (selector/dispatch core)
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md
- docs/developer-guide-status-integration.md

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: \"Should the intake agent ask clarifying questions interactively?\" — Answer (seed intent): \"No; do not ask questions.\" Final: no interactive questions.
- Q: \"What is the canonical work item id to use for this intake?\" — Answer (agent inference): \"W1\" mapped to AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU. Final: AM-0MNV3O3SF007DQYU.
- Q: \"Are measurable acceptance criteria provided?\" — Answer (agent inference): Not present in seed. Final: OPEN QUESTION - requester must supply or accept proposed criteria.


Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Scope creep if unstated requirements are assumed. Mitigation: record additional requests as linked work items.
- Assumption: Requester will respond if status=input_needed is set.

Related work (automated report)

- AM-0MNUB4NP0007QJ63 — Intake query and selection: selector logic that chose W1 for intake; useful to trace selection rationale.
- AM-0MNUZ6SEO008543G — Prior W1 intake: earlier run of the same non-interactive intake; useful precedent for handling missing acceptance criteria.
- docs/operator-guide-input-needed.md — Describes operator behaviour for items flagged input_needed; relevant policy for this intake.
